### 271-pigment-mosaic
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 285 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-02

Open `/dream/271-pigment-mosaic`. **The first AI-image-generation-INSIDE-an-AV-piece in the lab** (Karel's stated #1 direction, previously 0√ó in the last 15 builds) ‚ÄĒ and a deliberate **anti-glow**: an AI-generated "chapter" image is treated as reconfigurable *matter*, sliced into an N√óM grid of tiles that the live music assembles, shatters, scales and re-sorts in real time, so a picture is something the sound **builds and destroys** rather than a static backdrop. Pick a source (mic / drop a file / **Karel's Welcome Home piano by track ID** via `/api/audio/:id` JSON-or-bytes / a D-dorian synth fallback ‚ÄĒ all through one `fftSize 2048` analyser); the lower FFT is folded into 16 column-bands plus RMS, spectral centroid and spectral flux. **Summon chapter** maps the live mood ‚Üí a poetic prompt ‚Üí `flux/schnell` (guarded api route) ‚Üí the returned image is sliced and animated: loud bands grow / counter-rotate / fling their tiles outward (shatter) and punch *darker* (no additive glow ever); quiet passages let the matter settle back into the coherent picture; a flux transient triggers a debounced **brightness re-sort** so bright matter migrates and dark matter sinks. Chapters crossfade tile-by-tile; optional auto-regen capped at ~25s to bound FAL cost.

Single Canvas2D, ~60fps, zero per-frame allocation, pure `source-over` `drawImage` (the non-luminous "mosaic" output the 2026-06-02 jury explicitly asked for). **Degrades to zero API calls**: seeds with a procedural value-noise chapter on load; a 501 (no `FAL_KEY`) shows an amber notice and slices a procedural chapter instead, so the full shatter/settle/re-sort experience demos with no key. No canvas ‚Üí rose notice; no mic / bad track ‚Üí synth fallback. API route uses `guard` first (origin + rate-limit + daily quota over Karel's FAL budget). Refs: Refik Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* (data as pigment), David Hockney photo-collage "joiners", ACM IMX 2025 (DOI 10.1145/3706370.3727869, paywalled ‚ÄĒ cited from abstract). Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 3-builder fire; siblings `latent-breath` (WebGL displacement-warp of the chapter texture) + `dream-chapters` (WebGL optical-feedback *melting-memory* piece) banked build-verified in IDEAS.md. Reads `/api/audio/:id` only (no side effects); no new deps.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/271-pigment-mosaic/README.md`

### 279-tremor-score
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 289 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/279-tremor-score`. Press **Begin listening to the Earth** ‚ÄĒ a calm low drone fades in and the day's earthquakes are plotted quietly on an ink world map. This is the lab's **first piece driven by a live external API**: it fetches the USGS real-time earthquake GeoJSON feed (`all_day` / `all_hour`, CORS-open, no key) and turns every quake on Earth into a sound event *and* an ink mark, so the composition is literally about the world right now and **never exactly repeats**. **Magnitude** ‚Üí loudness + duration + a deep sub-rumble swell (M‚Č•2.2) and filtered low-noise rumble (M‚Č•4); **depth (km)** ‚Üí pitch register / timbre from a **just-intonation / overtone palette** over a 55 Hz root (0 km bright & high ‚Üí ~650 km dark, low, muffled ‚ÄĒ *not* C-major pentatonic, banned this cycle); **longitude** ‚Üí `StereoPanner` (W=left, E=right); **latitude** ‚Üí tone brightness + sawtooth for large events. Signal path per quake: osc(+sub/noise) ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí gain envelope ‚Üí StereoPanner ‚Üí master ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` limiter, with an always-on calm drone so the room is never silent. **Live** mode polls every 60s and sounds only genuinely-new quakes (staggered so a batch never slams); **Replay 24h** fast-plays the day in time-compressed order (24h ‚Üí ~90s).

Visual is **deliberately non-luminous** (the 2026-06-02 JURY's "ban the glow" verdict): a single Canvas2D in plain `source-over`, ink-on-paper on near-black ‚ÄĒ a graphite lat/long graticule + map, each quake an expanding ink ring that settles to a magnitude-sized dot (M‚Č•5 gets the one muted amber accent), and a scrolling pen-on-drum-paper **seismograph ribbon** that jolts per event. Degrades gracefully: if the USGS fetch fails (offline / CORS / blocked / empty) it falls back to a bundled ~30-quake set on a timer with a readable `text-amber-300/95` notice ‚ÄĒ **fully demoable with zero network**. No API route (client-side fetch to a keyless third-party feed; no secrets, no side effects), no new deps.

**First real-world-data sonification in the lab** ‚ÄĒ the JURY's explicitly-named empty shelf (the banked `transit-pulse`/`weather-score`/`iss-pass` seeds, finally realized via the cleanest keyless source). References: **SeismoDome** (Ben Holtzman, Seismic Sound Lab, Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory / AMNH), **seismic2midi** (PyPI, Nov 2025), USGS feeds. Born from RESEARCH ¬ß289. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-builder adult fire across three different empty shelves; siblings `midi-harmonograph` (first Web MIDI ‚ÄĒ harmony-as-harmonograph) + `ensemble-tabs` (first networked/multi-instance ‚ÄĒ BroadcastChannel ensemble) build-verified (all-three-present compiled) + banked in IDEAS.md.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/279-tremor-score/README.md`

### 280-kids-echo-canyon
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 290 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/280-kids-echo-canyon`. **For kids (4+).** Press **start singing**, then sing, hum, or call across the canyon ‚ÄĒ a little paper creature ("Echo") on the far cliff catches your phrase and sings it right back as a flight of colored paper birds, then layers a gentle harmony underneath. The lab's **first call-and-response / canon piece**: everything else in the lab reacts to sound or generates it; this one *listens to you, waits, and answers* ‚ÄĒ the oldest musical game there is, turned into a toy a four-year-old can play alone.

Four subsystems: (1) **mic + pitch detection** ‚ÄĒ `getUserMedia` ‚Üí `AnalyserNode` (analysis only; the voice is never played back, recorded, or sent), and ~every 130 ms a frame of time-domain samples runs through normalized **autocorrelation** pitch detection (Chris Wilson's canonical Web Audio method, YIN family, parabolic-interpolation refined); (2) **phrase segmentation** ‚ÄĒ an RMS gate captures degrees as you sing and ~620 ms of quiet ends the phrase, octave-collapsed so a high or low child voice both land in the same comfortable register; (3) **echo + harmony scheduler** ‚ÄĒ Echo replays your phrase note by note on a soft triangle mallet and adds a diatonic **third** 150 ms behind each note for a round-like shimmer, over an always-on C+G drone through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter (safe sounds, never silent); (4) **cut-paper canyon render** ‚ÄĒ rAF/refs-only, birds fly sine-arc beziers cliff to cliff, the singing creature bobs and opens its beak, color = pitch.

Built straight at the 2026-06-02 JURY's three demands: **non-pentatonic** (the scale is **C-Lydian** ‚ÄĒ its raised 4th floats, deliberately not the C-major pentatonic the lab kept defaulting to; no wrong notes ‚ÄĒ anything you sing is snapped into the mode), **non-luminous** (pure matte **cut-paper on Canvas2D** ‚ÄĒ flat dusk sky, paper cliffs with drop-shadow lips, a paper moon, birds as colored ovals; pure `source-over`, drop-shadows only, **no glow, no additive, no WebGL**), and it **audits the sound** (the mode is the point). **Input = mic** ‚ÄĒ chosen because `touch` is at √ó4 in the last-10 window (260/264/268/272) and therefore banned this cycle; mic was under-represented and is loved (`158-kids-hum-paint`‚Ě§ÔłŹ). Degrades gracefully: no mic / permission denied ‚Üí a **self-playing demo** where the two creatures sing call-and-response to each other on a timer (readable `text-rose-300` invite to allow the mic), so it's always demoable. No reading, no fail states, ‚Č•64px start button, ~14-min calm session. No API route, no `guard` needed (fully client-side; no server, no secrets).

Serves KIDS.md's explicitly-named **vocalization** goal (call-and-response is the purest singing prompt for 4yo language + music development) and the calm/contemplative corner KIDS.md stakes out. References: **YIN** (de Cheveign√© & Kawahara, JASA 2002), **Chris Wilson PitchDetect** (`cwilso/PitchDetect`; the lab already uses Wilson's two-clocks in `256`), **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (2005). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß290. Zero new deps ¬∑ zero API.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/280-kids-echo-canyon/README.md`

---

### 287-mirror-choir
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 295 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/287-mirror-choir`. Press **Begin**, allow the camera, and stand back ‚ÄĒ your whole body becomes a choir. This is the lab's **first body-tracking piece** (MediaPipe Pose Landmarker, 33 real-time 3D landmarks loaded at runtime from a CDN, no npm dep) and its **first vocal-formant synthesis** (Klatt-style: a sawtooth glottal pulse split through three parallel bandpass formants F1/F2/F3 per voice). Your left and right hands are two singing voices whose pitch follows a warm **D-Dorian** chord stack by their height in frame; **arm openness** (wrist-span √∑ shoulder-span) morphs the vowel continuously oo‚Üíoh‚Üíeh‚Üíah; **body height** shifts the register. Two softer pad voices + a low sine drone keep a chord under it all, through a delay tail ‚ÄĒ so it's never silent and never atonal (anything you do lands in the mode).

Deliberately **non-glow**: a matte **"wooden mirror"** (Daniel Rozin, *Wooden Mirror* 1999) ‚ÄĒ the camera is sampled into a grid of 14px tiles that light warm amber where your landmark-hull silhouette + pixel-brightness fall and stay near-black elsewhere; pure Canvas2D `source-over`, drop-shadows only, no additive/WebGL. Degrades gracefully: no camera / permission denied / MediaPipe fails ‚Üí a **"ghost dancer"** loops 7 hand-authored keyframe poses (smooth-stepped over 20s) that drive the exact same choir + mirror, with a readable `text-rose-300` notice ‚ÄĒ **fully demoable at 06:30 on a phone with no camera**. The mic is never touched (camera-only; nothing recorded). No API route, no `guard` needed (camera + CDN model + Web Audio are all client-side; no server, no secrets). Zero new deps.

**First MediaPipe / body-tracking + first formant synthesis in the lab** ‚ÄĒ the ambition floor's own example of a never-used technique. Born from RESEARCH ¬ß295. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire across three empty shelves; siblings `aurora-wire` (live NOAA space-weather sonification ‚ÄĒ the lab's 2nd real-world-data source) + `still-room` (eyes-closed HRTF spatial audio navigated by phone tilt ‚ÄĒ first non-screen + first DeviceOrientation controller) build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/287-mirror-choir/README.md`

### 290-kids-sound-safari
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 296 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/290-kids-sound-safari`. **For kids (4+).** Press the big **Start** button, then turn your whole body in a slow circle ‚ÄĒ six animals (frog, bird, whale, cricket, owl, bee) are hidden at fixed bearings *around* you in 3D sound, each humming a soft motif. When you turn to **face** one, its song swells to front-and-center and brightens, the phone gives a tiny `navigator.vibrate` buzz, the animal blooms big onto the mostly-dark screen and sings a happy phrase ‚ÄĒ "found!" ‚ÄĒ and joins the found-row. Find all six ‚Üí they sing a chord together, then it gently resets and loops. No reading, no fail, no timer; headphones make it magical, speakers still convey left/right.

This is the lab's **first deliberately non-screen / audio-FIRST piece** and its **first DeviceOrientation (heading) controller** in a shipped prototype ‚ÄĒ the screen is a footnote (a faint warm dusk-meadow compass + the found-row); the experience lives in spatial sound moving around the listener's head. Built straight at the 2026-06-03 JURY's provocations #2 (kids must *move* their body, not poke ‚ÄĒ touch input was banned this cycle), #3 (ship the spatial breadth banked-but-never-shipped ‚ÄĒ this is the `still-room` concept finally realized, recast for kids), #4 (build the unused head-tracked-spatial half of RESEARCH ¬ß295), and #5 (output is spatial *audio*, not a screen visualizer).

Subsystems: (1) **HRTF binaural panning** ‚ÄĒ one continuous `PannerNode` (`panningModel="HRTF"`, inverse distance) per animal at its compass bearing, plus an always-on ambient pad so it's never silent; (2) **DeviceOrientation head-tracking** ‚ÄĒ `alpha` rotates the `AudioListener` forward vector (feature-detects modern `forwardX/Z` AudioParams vs legacy `setOrientation`; same modern-vs-legacy detection for the panners), with iOS `requestPermission()` inside the click handler; (3) **find/collect state engine** ‚ÄĒ per-frame nearest-bearing test (within 25¬į) ‚Üí gain swell + lowpass brighten + bloom; first-find fires the sung phrase + vibrate + found-row; all-found ‚Üí celebration chord ‚Üí gentle auto-reset; (4) **minimal warm Canvas2D compass** render (no WebGL/three.js, no glow). Degrades gracefully: if no `deviceorientation` events arrive within ~2s (desktop / denied / no sensor), it falls back to **pointer-drag + ‚Üź ‚Üí arrow keys + a slow hands-free auto-tour** that finds the animals by itself with a readable `text-rose-300` hint ‚ÄĒ **fully demoable at 06:30 on a phone with zero sensors**. Mic is never opened; no recording. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + a motion sensor are all client-side; no server, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (2005); **Bernhard Leitner, *Sound Space*** (sound you move through); the **2026 head-tracked-spatial-audio wave** (THX Spatial Audio+ AI head tracking, CES Jan 2026 ‚ÄĒ the browser-feasible equivalent is `PannerNode` HRTF + DeviceOrientation, zero hardware). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß296. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `kids-shadow-dance` (camera frame-difference dancing ‚Üí blooming meadow) + `kids-sky-band` (today's real Open-Meteo weather ‚Üí a generative kids band) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS.md.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/290-kids-sound-safari/README.md`

### 291-harmonograph
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 297 ¬∑ **Cycle 2 (expressive)**: 299 ¬∑ **Cycle 3 (polychrome)**: 301 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

**Cycle 3 (cycle 301) ‚ÄĒ the polychrome specimen.** The multi-cycle thread reaches its planned 3rd cycle. The figure is no longer one color: **each held note draws its OWN running-composite thread in its circle-of-fifths color** (`pitchClassToColor`: `hue=((pc*7)%12)/12`, s 0.78 v 1.0 ‚Üí `hsvToRgb`), so a major triad visibly **weaves from three distinct-but-kindred hues** ‚ÄĒ and because a fifth is one constant hue step, the chord reads as a single family rather than three clashing colors (RESEARCH ¬ß301 chose circle-of-fifths over the naive chromatic-rainbow mapping for exactly this reason). New `sampleCompositeUpTo` sums pendulums `[0..i]` but normalizes by the FULL set's amplitude so partial threads stay spatially registered with the full figure; it still cleans up under Pure tuning because every composite is built from the same JI-snapped ratios. Added the lab's **first vector export**: **Export SVG** emits one aspect-corrected `<polyline>` per colored thread over a dark ground ‚Üí `harmonograph-<chord>.svg`, a true printable specimen (the PNG raster export from cycle 2 stays). A color **legend** mirrors the drawn threads (swatch + note name). References: **Chord Colourizer** (arXiv 2510.10173, 2025 ‚ÄĒ CQT chord ‚Üí Newton's color wheel), **Jack Ox**'s circle-of-fifths color/harmony wheel, **maddie lim**'s "12 Tone Color Theory." Build 9.29‚Üí**10.4 kB**. Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 2-approach fire; the parallel **scope-mode** explorer (continuous Pure‚áĄEqual XY phosphor vector-scope + beat-Hz/LOCKED readouts + presets, folding in the banked `phase-scope`, citing Lissajous/Jerobeam Fenderson/Ryoji Ikeda) is build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md as **cycle 4**. Build-verified, not browser-verified ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: multi-strip draw legibility on a dense chord, the SVG aspect projection, PNG readback on Safari.

**Cycle 2 (cycle 299) ‚ÄĒ the expressive live instrument.** The lab's first multi-cycle thread advances instead of starting fresh. Four performance layers now sculpt the figure as it draws, each with a MIDI control AND a no-hardware fallback: **sustain pedal ‚Üí figure-HOLD/accrete** (MIDI CC64 / Space bar / on-screen pad ‚ÄĒ released notes keep ringing AND keep contributing their decaying pendulum so the figure *accretes* as you layer chords over a held bass; lifting drops them from audio + figure together; HUD shows held-vs-pedaled, on-screen keys tint when pedaled); **mod-wheel ‚Üí pendulum damping** (MIDI CC1 / ‚ÜĎ‚Üď keys / slider ‚Üí exponential decay multiplier `0.35¬∑17^d`, loose-sprawl ‚Üí tight-spiral, with a readable `NN%` + word label); **velocity ‚Üí ink intensity** (avg held velocity drives a `uInk` shader uniform so dynamics are visible); **PNG export** (`preserveDrawingBuffer:true` + `canvas.toBlob` ‚Üí `harmonograph-<chord>.png`). Engine gained `HarmonographSynth.setPedal()` (parks key-released voices, returns dropped midis on release); the render loop composites held + pedaled notes. Build-verified (build size 7.51‚Üí**9.29 kB**), not browser-verified ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: pedal accrete edge cases (re-strike a parked note) and PNG readback on Safari (see STATE ¬ß299). Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 2-approach fire; the parallel `harmonograph-spectrum` explorer (per-note Newton-color-wheel threads + SVG vector specimen export, citing Chord Colourizer arXiv 2510.10173) is build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md as **cycle 3**.

Open `/dream/291-harmonograph`. Press **Start sound**, then play a chord ‚ÄĒ on a **MIDI keyboard**, your **computer keys** (`a w s e d f t g y h u j k o l p ;`, `z`/`x` = octave), or the **on-screen piano** ‚ÄĒ and the harmony *draws itself* as a Victorian **harmonograph**: each held note becomes a decaying pendulum whose frequency ratio is taken against the lowest held note, traced as `x(t)=ő£ aŠĶĘ¬∑sin(rŠĶĘt+ŌÜŠĶĘ)e^(‚ąídŠĶĘt)`, `y(t)=ő£ aŠĶĘ¬∑cos(rŠĶĘt+ŌÜŠĶĘ+kŠĶĘ)e^(‚ąídŠĶĘt)`. A prominent **Pure tuning (Just Intonation)** toggle snaps every ratio to the nearest small-integer just interval for BOTH the oscillator pitch AND the pendulum rates ‚ÄĒ so a consonant chord's figure visibly **cleans up into a near-closed spirograph** while the audible beating settles, at the same instant; under 12-TET the irrational ratios make the curve drift and tangle. That coupling ‚ÄĒ *see it close, hear it settle* ‚ÄĒ is the whole point.

Four subsystems: (1) **three-way note input ‚Üí one note-on/off path** ‚ÄĒ Web MIDI (`requestMIDIAccess({sysex:false})`, `onstatechange` hotplug, device-name readout), auto-repeat-guarded QWERTY, and a 2-octave on-screen piano (‚Č•44px, multi-touch pointer events); (2) **warm 12-voice Web Audio synth** (sine + +7¬Ę detuned triangle ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí ADSR ‚Üí shared feedback delay ‚Üí master ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` limiter, velocity ‚Üí loud+bright, soft always-on drone); (3) **JI-lock + chord/ratio analysis** (live HUD: held notes, best-guess chord name, active ratio set e.g. `1/1 : 5/4 : 3/2`); (4) **raw WebGL2 renderer** (NOT three.js, NOT Canvas2D) ‚ÄĒ hand-written GLSL ES 3.00, VAO/VBO, ~3000-point `LINE_STRIP` `bufferSubData`'d each frame, additive glow, translucent fade-quad ink trail, idle Lissajous "seed" figure when nothing is held, DPR/resize-aware. Optional **MIDI-out echo** (off by default) forwards held notes to the first output port, so the output isn't only a screen.

This is the lab's **first harmonograph / harmony-as-visible-geometry** piece, and it claims the **AMBITION-MANDATE criterion #4 (multi-cycle commitment) for the first time in the lab's history** ‚ÄĒ the 2026-06-03 JURY's #1 provocation (zero multi-cycle builds in 15+ cycles, "stop shipping orphans"). It is the banked, build-verified `281-midi-harmonograph` breadth resurrected (JURY provocation #3: "ship the breadth you've already paid for instead of citing new breadth"), and it inverts the audio‚Üívisual driver the JURY flagged (#5: output a MIDI/sound instrument, not a sound‚Üípicture viz). **Novelty honesty**: Web MIDI itself already exists at `4-operator` ‚ÄĒ the novel technique is the **harmonograph geometry** (0 prior hits in INDEX/READMEs, grep-verified), not MIDI. Diversity: INPUT=MIDI/keyboard ¬∑ OUTPUT=raw-WebGL2 line-geometry ¬∑ TECHNIQUE=harmonograph+JI ¬∑ VIBE=theory-literate live instrument ‚ÄĒ dodges the JURY's banned [touch ¬∑ three.js ¬∑ poke-toy-in-exotic-tuning] AND the over-represented [matte-Canvas2D 5√ó]. Degrades gracefully: no Web MIDI (e.g. Safari) ‚Üí amber notice + working keyboards; MIDI-but-no-device ‚Üí amber prompt; no WebGL2 ‚Üí rose notice (audio + keyboards still work). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + Web MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no recording, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps. References: the **harmonograph** (Hugh Blackburn pendulum apparatus, ~1840s) + **Lissajous figures** (J. A. Lissajous, 1857). Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 2-explorer adult fire; sibling **`phase-scope`** (a continuous Pure‚áĄEqual XY oscilloscope tuning-lock ‚ÄĒ see+hear consonance settle on three channels at once) build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. **Multi-cycle thread ‚ÄĒ cycle 2 (299) ADDED**: sustain-pedal figure-hold/accrete, mod-wheel‚Üípendulum-damping, velocity‚Üíink, PNG export (see top of this entry). **Cycle 3 will add**: per-note Newton-color-wheel threads (circle-of-fifths hue) + SVG vector specimen export + color legend (banked in IDEAS.md from the parallel explorer).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/291-harmonograph/README.md`

### 298-kids-echo-friend
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 302 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/298-kids-echo-friend`. **For kids (4+).** Press the big **"Sing to me ‚ú®"** button, then **sing any little phrase** ‚ÄĒ a soft glowing creature *listens*, **sings your phrase back** in its own warm voice, and **remembers** it. Every few rounds it plays a growing **little song** made of everything you've sung together. No reading, no timer, no score, no fail; the creature hums a gentle drone so it's never silent.

This is the kids lane's first **call-and-response with memory** ‚ÄĒ the antidote to the lane's local minimum ("poke a cute thing ‚Üí it drones, no memory"). It reframes the banked `294-kids-voice-garden` **out of the C-pentatonic rut** (quantizes to **D-Dorian**) and moves `296-kids-firefly-tilt`'s remember-and-replay mechanic onto a **fresher input** (voice, not tilt) ‚ÄĒ exactly the two queued kids moves from STATE ¬ß301.

Four subsystems into one pipeline: (1) **real-time monophonic pitch detection** ‚ÄĒ YIN/NSDF (normalized square difference) autocorrelation on raw `AnalyserNode` PCM, no ML, no CDN model, mic analysis-only (never recorded/uploaded), each detected Hz snapped to D-Dorian; (2) **call-and-response memory engine** ‚ÄĒ collects a phrase frame-by-frame, detects ~0.8‚Äď1.2 s of silence as phrase-end, dedupes into notes, pushes to a growing memory, and every 3rd phrase replays the whole accumulated song; (3) **warm sing-back synth** ‚ÄĒ sine + triangle through lowpass + short delay, drone-duck while echoing, all through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so it can never get loud/harsh; (4) **hand-written WebGL2 GLSL** creature ‚ÄĒ an SDF blob with domain-warped organic jiggle on an aurora ground, pitch-driven hue (violet‚Üíamber‚Üíteal‚Üílime across the Dorian range), one orbiting **memory orb per remembered phrase**, and a sing-back pulse ring. Degrades gracefully: **no mic / permission denied ‚Üí a hands-free auto-demo** sings 6 hand-authored D-Dorian phrases through the *identical* pitch‚Üíecho‚Üímemory pipeline (so the whole loop demos itself on a phone with no mic), with a readable `text-rose-300` notice; **no WebGL2 ‚Üí rose notice, audio still runs**; iOS-safe (mic + AudioContext created inside the click handler). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 are client-side; no recording, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **SingingSDS** (arXiv:2511.20972, Nov 2025 ‚ÄĒ a dialogue agent that *responds by singing*; this is its fully-client, no-AI, kid-sized embodiment), **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (the listen-then-answer tradition), and the classic **call-and-response / "Simon"** memory game. Born from RESEARCH ¬ß302. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `299-kids-clap-band` (clap ‚Üí onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) + `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow ‚Üí breath-envelope sailing, Wiener-entropy breath/tone discriminator) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS.md. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether YIN pitch-tracking on a real 4-year-old's wobbly voice lands cleanly on the Dorian grid, and whether the sing-back-and-grow loop reads as a "song we're building together" vs. a list of echoes.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/298-kids-echo-friend/README.md`

### 303-kids-wind-harp
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 304 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/303-kids-wind-harp`. **For kids (4+).** Hit the big amber **"Tilt to play ‚Ėł"** button, then **tilt your phone or iPad** ‚ÄĒ gravity swings a row of seven glowing strings like a wind-harp, and any string that swings far enough **plucks itself and sings**. The child plays music by *tipping the world*, hands-free; no tapping, no reading, no timer, no fail.

The lab's **first tilt-input piece since the loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`**, and a deliberate pull on three loved prototypes (`83-kids-tilt-rain`‚Ě§ÔłŹ tilt ¬∑ `105-pluck-field`‚Ě§ÔłŹ Karplus pluck ¬∑ `140-kids-string-bridge`‚Ě§ÔłŹ playable strings). It couples two genuinely **fresh-for-this-lab techniques**: (1) **Verlet-integration string physics** ‚ÄĒ each of 7 strings is a 14-node mass-chain solved with Verlet + distance-constraint relaxation (3 passes/frame) on a **fixed 120 Hz step**; the device-tilt `deviceorientation` ő≤/ő≥ becomes a **gravity vector**, so tipping the tablet changes which way "down" is and the hanging strings swing and settle with believable physical motion; (2) **Karplus-Strong plucked-string synthesis** (Karplus & Strong, 1983) ‚ÄĒ when a string's midpoint swing crosses a threshold it plucks: a noise burst rendered offline through a tuned lowpass-fed delay line into an `AudioBuffer`, bigger swing ‚Üí louder/brighter/longer, per-string refractory so it never machine-guns, stereo-panned low‚Üíhigh. A third subsystem is the hand-written **raw WebGL2** ribbon renderer (matte alpha-over string curves expanded along their normals on a soft breathing dark gradient, a glow flash per pluck ‚ÄĒ **no additive bloom, no three.js**). The 7 strings are tuned to **D-Dorian** (D E F G A B C ‚ÄĒ explicitly NOT C-major-pentatonic), with an ambient D+A drone underneath and a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so simultaneous plucks can never blast.

Degrades at every step so it's **fully playable on a desktop with no sensors**: iOS 13+ `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start gesture (denial ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` note + fallback); **pointer-drag** tilts the gravity vector on desktop; and a **2 s auto-sway** kicks in when no tilt events arrive so the harp **plays itself** and a 4-year-old (or Karel at 06:30 on his phone) always sees cause‚Üíeffect. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the **Aeolian harp** (a stringed instrument played by moving air rather than fingers ‚ÄĒ here the child's tilt is the wind); the **Karplus-Strong** plucked-string algorithm (1983). Chains from RESEARCH ¬ß304 (*Rhythm in the Air*, arXiv:2511.00793, Nov 2025 ‚ÄĒ embodied motion ‚Üí scale-constrained musical output; tilt is the body, D-Dorian is the scale constraint). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `299-kids-clap-band` (clap ‚Üí HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) + `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow ‚Üí Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing) build-reviewed and re-banked in IDEAS.md. **Ambition 2/5**: never-used-technique(#1, Verlet rope physics) + named-reference(#3). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: the deviceorientation‚Üígravity *feel* and pluck threshold on a real tilting hand, the iOS permission branches, and the rendered look of the ribbon strips (no sensors/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/303-kids-wind-harp/README.md`

### 306-kids-rain-shaker
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 306 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker`. **For kids (4+).** Tap the big amber **"Shake to play"** button, then **shake the phone or iPad like a rainstick or maraca** ‚ÄĒ gentle shakes make a soft trickle of beads, bigger shakes tumble a warm rain down the screen, and each strong shake-peak strikes a **D-Dorian** bell. The child plays by *moving the whole device*, hands-free; no tapping, no reading, no timer, no fail. An always-on pad keeps it alive, and a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter means even the most vigorous shaking can never blast.

The lab's **first `devicemotion` / accelerometer instrument** in 300+ prototypes ‚ÄĒ and deliberately a *different sensor and axis* from the lab's prior motion pieces: `303-kids-wind-harp` and `290-kids-sound-safari` used `deviceorientation` (tilt ő≤/ő≥ and compass heading őĪ ‚ÄĒ device *orientation*), whereas this reads `accelerationIncludingGravity` for shake *energy*. Three subsystems: (1) **`shake.ts`** ‚ÄĒ per `devicemotion` event, read the acceleration vector, subtract a slow per-axis running average (a **high-pass** that removes the constant gravity component), take the magnitude, and feed a smoothed **shake-energy envelope** (fast attack, slow release); threshold crossings with a ~130 ms refractory emit discrete **hit** events while the continuous envelope drives bead density. Every input path (motion, pointer-shake, auto-demo) feeds the IDENTICAL `pushSample` pipeline. (2) **`rain-audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ a soft D-Dorian pad drone (detuned triangles, breathing lowpass) + a rainstick trickle of short filtered-noise bead grains whose spawn rate/brightness track the live energy + **FM bell chimes** struck on hit events (panned, velocity from hit strength, lowpassed so they never get piercing), all summed through a final `DynamicsCompressor` used as a limiter. (3) **`rain-gl.ts`** ‚ÄĒ raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): a soft **dark‚Üídawn** gradient quad that warms with energy + a CPU-simulated pool of up to 600 falling **bead/rain particles** as soft point-sprites in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style ‚ÄĒ switched from the builder's additive blend during curation to honor the JURY's anti-glow ban) + warm glow blooms on each bell. DPR/resize aware.

Degrades for the 06:30 phone review: iOS 13+ `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start tap (denial ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` note + fallback); on a sensor-less device it falls back to **pointer/mouse-shake** (fast pointer movement synthesises an acceleration vector into the same detector) and a gentle **auto-demo** that rains and chimes by itself; no WebGL2 ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice, audio still runs. `AudioContext` is created inside the Start tap (iOS-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + a motion sensor + WebGL2 are all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the **rainstick** (Andean/Chilean cactus-spine instrument played by tilting and shaking) + the **maraca / shaker** percussion tradition; the embodied-motion‚Üísound mapping echoes movement-sonification research (**CHI 2026**, *Designing Interactive Movement Sonification*). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß306. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `304-kids-clap-band` (clap ‚Üí HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*, D-Mixolydian) + `305-kids-blow-sail` (blow ‚Üí Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing, C-Lydian) build-verified and re-banked in IDEAS.md. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, devicemotion shake-energy) + named-reference(#3) + recent-research(#5, ¬ß306). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: the shake threshold/scale *feel* on a real shaking hand (one-number fixes if too touchy or too stiff), the auto-demo chime cadence, and WebGL2 point-size behaviour across GPUs (no phone sensor / GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker/README.md`

### 371-kids-clap-along
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 336 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/371-kids-clap-along`. **For kids (4+).** Tap the big amber **"Clap to play ‚Ėł"** button, then **clap your real hands** ‚ÄĒ a friendly creature claps a short rhythm, you clap it back, and **the phone HEARS your claps through the microphone** (no screen-tapping). On a good-enough match the creature delights and the shared rhythm **grows by one clap** ‚ÄĒ Simon-style ‚ÄĒ so the little song the two of you share gets longer and longer. No reading, no score, no fail; a missed clap-back just warmly replays the same pattern ("let's try together").

The lab's **first prototype that actually LISTENS for a real hand-clap.** A grep audit found the lab has shipped call-and-response/echo rhythm **five times** (`154-kids-clap-back`, `213-kids-echo-drum`, `209-kids-drum-tap`, `150-kids-beat-builder`, `218-kids-xylophone-drops`) ‚ÄĒ and **every one is TOUCH** (the device plays a pattern, the child taps the screen back). None hear a real clap; onset detection itself was banked-but-never-shipped (`299`/`304-kids-clap-band`). 371 finally does it. Four subsystems: (1) **`onset.ts`** ‚ÄĒ real-time **acoustic onset detection**: each animation frame it reads the mic's FFT from an `AnalyserNode` (`getFloatFrequencyData`, `fftSize` 1024, `smoothingTimeConstant` 0 so transients aren't blurred), computes a **spectral-flux √ó high-frequency-content (HFC)** novelty (half-wave-rectified positive bin-to-bin energy increases, weighted toward high bins so a bright broadband clap pops while the steady drone/voice stays low), compares it to an **adaptive threshold** (slow running mean √ó slack + floor, so it self-tunes to a loud kitchen or quiet bedroom), and applies a **~140 ms refractory window** so one clap ‚ÄĒ even a wobbly toddler double-bounce ‚ÄĒ counts once. (2) **`pattern.ts`** ‚ÄĒ a **growing-memory call-and-response state machine** (`intro ‚Üí calling ‚Üí waiting ‚Üí judging ‚Üí celebrate`): the creature calls a rhythm, the child's detected onset times are greedily matched against the target beats within a generous **¬Ī0.34 s** tolerance, ~60% lands = a match ‚Üí celebrate + **grow the pattern by one beat** (occasionally a syncopation); a miss costs nothing. The growing shared rhythm IS the song ‚ÄĒ the **memory/consequence** the jury asked the kids lane for after `350-kids-bump-along` regressed to a memory-less poke-toy. (3) **`clap-audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ an always-on soft **D + A drone** + **D-Dorian** tuned clap voices (D E F G A B C ‚ÄĒ explicitly NOT C-major-pentatonic), call panned/brightened one way and the child's answer the other (two voices), beats tuned up the scale by position so the growing rhythm reads as a little tune, a rising reward sparkle on each grow, all through a `DynamicsCompressor` hard limiter so it can never blast. (4) **`creature-gl.ts`** ‚ÄĒ raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): one warm **breathing creature** (squashes when it claps, leans in to listen on the child's turn, eyes curve into happy arcs when delighted) above a **row of glowing clap beads** that grows with the song so the child can *see* it getting longer ‚ÄĒ amber for the call, mint for the answer ‚ÄĒ in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style, no glow-stacking).

One unified pipeline, three doors: every input route is judged by the **identical** onset machine ‚ÄĒ **mic** (real acoustic claps via `sampleMic()`), **pointer/tap clap** (mic denied ‚Üí a tap plays a **real broadband noise burst** into the *same* analyser so it's detected exactly like a hand clap), and **auto-demo** (no claps for ~4.5 s ‚Üí the creature calls AND claps its own answer back through the same `clap()‚Üíanalyser‚Üídetector` path, so the piece **plays itself hands-free** for the 06:30 phone review). Degrades at every step: `AudioContext` + `getUserMedia({audio})` created **inside the Start tap** (iOS-safe); mic is analysis-only (never recorded/uploaded, analyser not routed to the speakers ‚Üí no feedback); mic denied ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice + tap-to-clap fallback; no WebGL2 ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice, audio still runs. No API route, no `guard` needed (mic-analysis + WebGL2 all client-side; no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Drumball** (Audio Mostly 2024 ‚ÄĒ a tangible call-and-response drumming system inspired by the West African djembe) ¬∑ **Steve Reich, *Clapping Music* (1972)** ¬∑ the African call-and-response / talking-drum tradition. Born from RESEARCH ¬ß336 (lab-gap audit: 5√ó clap-back, all touch; 0√ó real mic clap). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `372-kids-blow-garden` (blow ‚Üí Wiener-entropy breath-envelope growing garden) + `373-kids-firefly-path` (tap-to-lay-stones ‚Üí firefly replays a growing D-Dorian path) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß336. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, acoustic clap onset detection) + ‚Č•3-subsystems(#2) + named-reference(#3). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: the onset thresholds/HFC weighting on a real mic (too-hot self-trigger vs. too-deaf soft claps; defaults lean forgiving), drone-vs-mic self-noise under real AGC, the ¬Ī0.34 s / ~60% match tolerance against a real toddler clap-back, and the WebGL2 creature/bead sizing (no mic / GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/371-kids-clap-along/README.md`

### 375-tempo-canon
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 337 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/375-tempo-canon`. **The accompanist that follows YOU.** Press the big violet **"Play demo ‚Ėł"** button (or play it yourself on the home-row keys / a Web MIDI keyboard): you play the melody of Beethoven's **Ode to Joy** (the right hand), and the system aligns your live performance to the reference score in real time and plays the **bass + chord accompaniment (the left hand) locked to your tempo** ‚ÄĒ speeding up when you rush, slowing when you ritard. The signature is that you can *literally see the machine following you*: the **warping path** ‚ÄĒ a glowing diagonal that bends steeper when you rush and flattens when you drag ‚ÄĒ is the centerpiece visual.

The JURY's **#1 standing adult ask, finally taken**: a real-time score-follower / live-accompaniment tool. Score-following had **lost curation three times** (re-rejected cycle 335) for three specific reasons, and this build is a deliberate answer to each: (a) *"not lab-first ‚ÄĒ `26-score-follow` exists"* ‚Üí this is the live **accompanist** (it *plays the accompaniment in sync with your rubato*, a different deliverable from `26`'s bare cursor), and its **online-DTW warping-path render is genuinely lab-first** (grep: no prior prototype draws an alignment path); (b) *"needs mic INPUT, which the JURY bans"* ‚Üí it is driven by **MIDI / computer-keyboard / a baked demo, NO microphone**, dodging the ban entirely; (c) *"real-instrument verifiability wall a sandbox can't cross ‚ÄĒ the thing that killed it 3√ó"* ‚Üí solved exactly the way `358-beat-mirror` solved it, with a **built-in known performance** (see below) that self-proves the whole pipeline with zero hardware. The JURY's own provocation #1 prescribed precisely this ("solve it the way 358 did, with a known internal answer").

Four subsystems: (1) **`dtw.ts`** ‚ÄĒ **online / streaming Dynamic Time Warping** (Simon Dixon's MATCH lineage, 2005): instead of a full N√óM offline matrix it grows the alignment path one note at a time inside a **bounded search window** (`radius 5`) ahead of the last committed column, via the classic 3-predecessor recurrence `D(i,j)=cost(i,j)+min(D(i-1,j),D(i,j-1),D(i-1,j-1))`, keeping only the current/previous frontier rows; commits are **forward-only** (position never jumps backward) so the accompaniment can't stutter. **Local tempo = the slope of the warping path** (steeper than 45¬į = rushing, shallower = dragging). (2) **input layer** ‚ÄĒ one `handlePlayedNote` pipe fed by three doors: the baked **demo**, **computer keyboard** (home-row `a s d f g h j k` ‚Üí D-major scale, labels shown), and **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess()`, graceful status if absent ‚ÄĒ never throws). (3) **`audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ Web Audio synthesis only: a detuned-oscillator piano voice with a fast-attack/expo-decay env; an optional soft **melody echo** + the star **accompaniment** (functional bass + chord triad in D major, I/V/vi/IV) whose chord decay scales with the slope-derived tempo, so the left hand rings longer when you drag. (4) **`gl.ts`** ‚ÄĒ raw **WebGL2** with custom shaders plotting reference-score-time (X) √ó your-performance-time (Y): the glowing **bending warping path**, the faint **45¬į in-step guide**, the translucent **bounded search window** band, a pulsing **current-cell cursor**, and expanding **accompaniment fire-rings**, one per chord.

The headline verifiability feature is the **built-in known performance** (`makePerformance()` in `score.ts`): a baked Ode-to-Joy with expressive rubato ‚ÄĒ a steady intro, an **accelerando** through the middle, a **ritardando** into the final held note, plus deterministic per-note timing jitter ‚ÄĒ emitted note-by-note on wall-clock timers. The follower never sees the score positions; it only receives pitches over time and must align them. So pressing one button proves the whole pipeline **with no microphone, no MIDI device, no camera**: you watch the warping path bend (steepening in the accelerando, flattening in the ritard), the BPM readout swing, and the accompaniment lock on. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice, follower + audio still run. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Matchmaker ‚ÄĒ *An Open-source Library for Real-time Piano Score Following and Systematic Evaluation*** (arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025; systematically compares online-DTW vs HMM followers) ¬∑ **Simon Dixon, *Live Tracking of Musical Performances Using On-Line Time Warping* / MATCH** (2005). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß337. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept, three follower algorithms); siblings `374-the-accompanist` (HMM/forward note-event follower + confidence meter, score-river viz) + `376-the-prompter` (cue/anchor FSM robust to wrong notes, build-time-proven 8/8 cues) build-verified and banked in IDEAS ¬ß337 as the winner's **next-cycle deepenings**. **Ambition 3/5**: ‚Č•3-subsystems(#2, input + online-DTW follower + Web-Audio elastic accompaniment + WebGL2 warping-path) + named-reference(#3, Matchmaker/Dixon) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, "The Accompanist" thread ‚ÄĒ cycle-1 ships alignment+warping-path+self-verifying demo; cycle-2 folds in the HMM/confidence follower behind a DTW/HMM toggle + ACCompanion-style expressive dynamics following; cycle-3 maps Karel's own *Welcome Home* melody as the score). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether a real fumbly performance keeps the forward-only DTW aligned (a long string of wrong notes could outrun the window-5 search before re-locking), the slope‚Üítempo blend feel on real rubato, the warping-path legibility at a 06:30 phone glance, and real Web MIDI on hardware (no MIDI/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/375-tempo-canon/README.md`

### 380-expressive-accompanist
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 339 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/380-expressive-accompanist`. **The accompanist that follows your EXPRESSION, not just your tempo.** Press the big violet **"Play demo ‚Ėł"** (or play the home-row keys / a Web MIDI keyboard): you play the melody (right hand) of **Pachelbel's Canon in D**, and the system plays the **bass + chord accompaniment (left hand)** that follows you in **three dimensions at once** ‚ÄĒ it speeds/slows with your *tempo*, swells/fades with your *dynamics* (play soft ‚Üí it answers soft), and detaches/sustains with your *articulation* (tap staccato ‚Üí its chords go short and crisp; hold legato ‚Üí they ring). Cycle 1 of this thread (`375-tempo-canon`) followed only your tempo; this is the deepening that makes the machine feel like a duet partner instead of a backing track.

**Cycle 2 of the lab's "Accompanist" multi-cycle thread**, and the most direct realization of **The ACCompanion**'s central thesis (Cancino-Chac√≥n/Peter/Widmer, IJCAI 2023): *tempo alone sounds mechanical ‚ÄĒ following dynamics and articulation is what makes an accompanist human.* The lab-first axis is **expressive-parameter coupling**: a grep of every prior README shows no prototype that *follows a soloist's dynamics or articulation* (375 follows only tempo). Three subsystems past the input layer: (1) **`dtw.ts`** ‚ÄĒ online/streaming **DTW** (Simon Dixon's MATCH, 2005): a bounded forward-only warping path (radius ‚Čą5, 3-predecessor recurrence `D(i,j)=cost+min(D(i-1,j),D(i,j-1),D(i-1,j-1))`, frontier rows only) gives *which* score position you're at and a local-tempo slope ‚ÄĒ extended in cycle 2 with two EMA trackers: `smoothedVelocity` (őĪ 0.35) and `smoothedArticulation` = note-held-duration √∑ inter-onset-interval (őĪ 0.30; ~1 = legato, ~0 = staccato). (2) **`audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ Web Audio synthesis only: a detuned-oscillator piano melody echo + the star **accompaniment** (functional bass + chord triads, Pachelbel ground bass I-V-vi-III-IV-I-IV-V) whose **loudness scales with the followed dynamics**, **decay length scales with the followed articulation**, and **chord placement is driven by the DTW tempo**, all summed through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast. (3) **`gl.ts`** ‚ÄĒ raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): the cycle-1 warping path, now wearing an **expression ribbon** ‚ÄĒ a halo layer whose **point-sprite size encodes live dynamics** (fat glow = loud) and whose **screen-space dashing (`u_dash` uniform) encodes articulation** (solid = legato, dotted = staccato), over a solid cyan-white alignment core; plus a translucent search-window band, a 45¬į in-step guide, a pulsing committed-cell cursor, and expanding **fire-rings** on each chord whose size = that chord's loudness. DPR/resize aware.

One unified `handlePlayedNote(pitch, velocity, durationMs)` pipe, three doors: the baked **demo** (default; auto-plays ~2 s after load so the 06:30 phone reviewer sees + hears all three couplings hands-free), **computer keyboard** (home-row `a s d f g h j k` ‚Üí a D-major scale, labels shown; hold for legato, tap for staccato), and **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess()`, graceful status if absent ‚ÄĒ never throws; real velocity drives dynamics). The headline verifiability move (the thing that killed score-following 3√ó before `375` solved it the `358-beat-mirror` way): the baked **"known performance"** (`makePerformance()` in `score.ts`) bakes deliberately wide, *legible* swings in all three axes ‚ÄĒ a steady intro ‚Üí **accelerando** ‚Üí big **ritardando**; a **crescendo** (pp‚Üíff) then **diminuendo** (ff‚Üímp); a **legato** first half (ratio ~0.85) then a **staccato** second half (ratio ~0.25). The follower never sees score positions ‚ÄĒ only `(pitch, velocity, durationMs, dt)` tuples ‚ÄĒ so pressing one button proves the whole tempo+dynamics+articulation pipeline **with no MIDI, no mic, no camera**: the ribbon visibly swells then thins, dashes then re-solidifies, and the accompaniment audibly swells/fades and detaches/sustains to match. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice, follower + audio still run; no Web MIDI ‚Üí status line, keyboard + demo still work; `AudioContext` created inside the Play tap (autoplay-safe, silent catch). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **The ACCompanion** ‚ÄĒ Cancino-Chac√≥n, Peter & Widmer, *Combining Reactivity, Robustness, and Musical Expressivity in an Automatic Piano Accompanist* (IJCAI 2023, arXiv:2304.12939; the expressive-coupling north star) ¬∑ **Peransformer** ‚ÄĒ score-aware-discriminator expressive performance rendering (arXiv:2510.10175, Oct 2025) ¬∑ **Simon Dixon, MATCH** online-DTW (2005). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß339. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *an accompanist that follows your expression* ‚ÄĒ three technical attacks); siblings `381-resilient-accompanist` (dual DTW‚áĄHMM follower with confidence-gated handoff that survives wrong/skipped notes) + `382-anticipating-accompanist` (Kalman-style predictive tempo model that schedules the accompaniment *onto your predicted next onset* so it plays *with* you, not behind) build-verified and banked in IDEAS ¬ß339 as the winner's **cycle-3 deepenings** (fold robustness + anticipation into the expressive follower). **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, expressive-parameter following ‚ÄĒ dynamics + articulation, lab-first) + named-reference(#3, ACCompanion/Peransformer/Dixon) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, "The Accompanist" thread cycle 2; cycle-3 folds in 381's robustness + 382's anticipation; cycle-4 maps Karel's own *Welcome Home* melody as the score). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the EMA articulation ratio reads cleanly off real (vs baked) playing, the dynamics/decay coupling *feel* through the limiter when chords overlap, the `u_dash` staccato dashing + velocity-sized fire-rings render as intended across GPUs, and real Web MIDI velocity on hardware (no MIDI/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/380-expressive-accompanist/README.md`

### 384-kids-firefly-chorus
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 340 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-07 ¬∑ **For**: kids (4+)

Open `/dream/384-kids-firefly-chorus`. **Tip the world to gather scattered fireflies until they spontaneously blink ‚ÄĒ and sing ‚ÄĒ in unison.** A dark dusk meadow holds ~280 fireflies, each blinking out of phase and humming its own slightly-different soft note (a gentle twinkly chaos). **Tilt the device** (or drag a finger; or just watch) to drift fireflies together ‚ÄĒ and when they cluster they begin to *nudge each other's blink timing* until a gathered group locks into a single pulsing blink and its scattered pitches collapse onto one shared D-Dorian chord. The child *feels chaos becoming harmony* by gathering them; clusters that have synced stay synced. No "wrong", no fail, no score ‚ÄĒ a meadow that breathes into unison.

The lab-first axis is **spontaneous synchronization** itself: a grep of every prior README shows fireflies as a decorative motif (`122-kids-firefly-song`, `211-kids-firefly-web`, `188-kids-glow-bug`) but **no prototype implements coupled-oscillator phase-locking** ‚ÄĒ the *emergence* of order from disorder has never been the mechanic. Three subsystems: (1) **`kuramoto.ts`** ‚ÄĒ a genuine **Kuramoto model**: each firefly is a phase oscillator integrating `dőłŠĶĘ/dt = ŌČŠĶĘ + (K/NŠĶĘ)¬∑ő£‚Īľ sin(őł‚Īľ ‚ąí őłŠĶĘ)` summed over **only its neighbours** within a coupling radius (cheap **spatial-hash grid**, 3√ó3 cell window, ~280 oscillators at 60fps), flashing when őł crosses 2ŌÄ; **union-find** clusters the locked groups; it computes both a **per-firefly local order parameter** and the **global Kuramoto order parameter r‚ąą[0,1]** (`r¬∑e^{iŌą} = (1/N)¬∑ő£ e^{iőł‚Īľ}`), surfaced as a legible "togetherness" meter (r‚Čą0 chaos ‚Üí r‚Čą1 unison) ‚ÄĒ the synchronization made *visible*, in the legible/instructional register the jury asked the lab to feed. (2) **`audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ Web Audio only: an always-on soft D+A summer-night pad bed (never silent) + **D-Dorian** sine bells (D E F G A B C ‚ÄĒ explicitly **not** C-major-pentatonic) that, as a neighbourhood's local r rises, blend toward the shared **D-F-A tonic triad**; a 14-voice pool + per-firefly refractory + probability gate so hundreds of flashes never machine-gun; a low-pass tone and a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast; a 12-min goodnight fade. (3) **`gl.ts`** ‚ÄĒ raw **WebGL2** (GLSL ES 3.00): a dusk-indigo meadow gradient (warm vignette tracks global r) + instanced soft-glow firefly quads in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style), DPR/resize aware.

One sim, three input doors, all converging on the same model: **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission()` requested inside the Start tap) ‚Üí a gravity vector that drifts the fireflies; a **pointer-drag** "breeze" fallback that drifts them identically when orientation is denied/absent; and an **auto-demo** ‚ÄĒ after ~3s with no input a slow synthetic wandering breeze herds the meadow into one cluster, so the 06:30 phone reviewer *sees the blinks lock and the togetherness meter climb and hears the chord settle* without touching anything or needing a sensor. Degrades cleanly: orientation denied ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice + drag fallback + auto-demo still runs; no WebGL2 ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice (audio still runs); `AudioContext` created inside the Start tap (autoplay-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (DeviceOrientation + WebGL2 + Web Audio all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Yoshiki Kuramoto (1975)** ‚ÄĒ the coupled-phase-oscillator model ¬∑ **Christiaan Huygens (1665)** ‚ÄĒ the original "sympathy of clocks" coupled-pendulum synchronization ¬∑ the spontaneous flash-synchrony of **SE-Asian *Pteroptyx* fireflies** ¬∑ live research front: **arXiv:2603.08352** ("Synchronization of higher-dimensional Kuramoto oscillators on networks," March 2026). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß340. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire (three unrelated emergent-system sound worlds, each dodging the jury's banned tags ‚ÄĒ touch / mic-voice / inline-SVG); siblings `383-kids-coral-garden` (shake ‚Üí **Diffusion-Limited Aggregation** coral growth, Witten‚ÄďSander 1981) + `385-kids-ant-garden` (wave at camera ‚Üí **ant-colony stigmergy** pheromone-trail song, Deneubourg 1990 / Dorigo 1992) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß340 ‚ÄĒ both grep-verified lab-first techniques. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, coupled-oscillator synchronization) + named-reference(#3, Kuramoto/Huygens) + recent-research(#5, arXiv:2603.08352, Mar 2026). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: the coupling constant K (3.4) + radius (0.13) feel on a real cluster (too strong ‚Üí instant lock reads as no-emergence; too weak ‚Üí never syncs), the tilt‚Üígravity mapping on a real tilting hand, whether the sync reads as *magic* vs. a glitch at a 06:30 phone glance, and the voice-pool load when many neighbourhoods flash at once (no sensor/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/384-kids-firefly-chorus/README.md`

### 387-drop-engine
**Status**: `demoable` ¬∑ **Cycle shipped**: 341 ¬∑ **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/387-drop-engine`. **The lab's first EDM / club journey engine ‚ÄĒ a generative build-and-drop arc you can feel.** Tap **START** and a continuous club track composes itself through the canonical EDM tension structure ‚ÄĒ **GROOVE ‚Üí BUILD (riser + low-pass filter sweep + accelerating snare-roll + a high-pass "suck-out" right before the impact) ‚Üí DROP (a beat of silence, then the full kick+sub+supersaw hit at peak energy) ‚Üí RELEASE/breakdown ‚Üí loop** ‚ÄĒ 16 bars per phase at 126 BPM, re-seeding its variation each loop (riser type, lead-motif transposition, snare-fill pattern) so no two arcs are identical. **HOLD ‚Üí CHARGE** biases how hard the next drop hits; **DROP NOW** triggers the impact early during a build. It runs the whole arc hands-free after the Start tap, so the 06:30 phone reviewer hears a full build-and-drop without touching anything.

**Why it matters / what's new:** this directly answers Karel's standing **priority #4 ‚ÄĒ journey-engine alternatives** ("EDM build-and-drop, ritual, jazz responsive, cinematic narrative‚Ä¶ as alternate arcs"), which the lab had **never** delivered: every prior adult piece is either ambient (pads, drones, granular textures) or analytical (DTW score-following, key/cadence analysis). A grep of every prior README confirms **no prior prototype is an EDM/club arc or a build-drop tension engine** ‚ÄĒ this is a genuinely new *vibe* and a new *engine*, the maximal-diversity break from the adult "too similar" monoculture (three slow JI-drones + five legible-analysis pieces) the jury flagged. The central idea is a **single tension scalar ‚ąą [0,1]** as a designer-accessible "knob" (the Procedural-Music-Generation pattern): during BUILD it ramps and *simultaneously* drives the riser pitch+gain, the bass low-pass cutoff sweep, the snare-roll note-spacing halving, the pre-impact high-pass suck-out, and every visual ‚ÄĒ so the arc is **legible in both sound and picture** (the jury's "feed the legible lane," applied to *form/energy* instead of harmonic analysis).

Four subsystems: (1) **`arc.ts`** ‚ÄĒ the clock-driven **state machine** (GROOVE/BUILD/DROP/RELEASE over bars, the tension computation, per-loop variation seeds, derived helpers `riserCutoff`/`suckoutHP`/`bassCutoff`/`snareRollSpacing`); (2) **`synth.ts`** ‚ÄĒ a **multi-layer Web-Audio synth+drum engine** (kick = pitch-enveloped sine + click; snare/clap = filtered noise burst; hat = high-passed noise; bass = saw + sub through a tension-swept low-pass; **supersaw lead** = 5 detuned oscillators on a D-minor/Dorian motif; the rising-noise/saw riser), everything summed through a master **`DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter** (threshold ‚ąí6 dB, ratio 12) + master gain so even the drop can never clip or blast; (3) **`scheduler.ts`** ‚ÄĒ a **look-ahead beat scheduler** (a 25 ms `setInterval` poll that schedules notes ~100 ms ahead of `audioCtx.currentTime`, per-phase beat patterns, phase-entry hooks) for tight timing independent of rAF; (4) **`viz.ts`** ‚ÄĒ raw **WebGL2** (tension-arc ring, spring-physics frequency bars that pile up through the build, a **particle bloom on the DROP**, a soft beat-flash), additive blending, DPR/resize aware, soft radial glow rather than harsh full-white strobes (seizure-safe). Steering + HUD live in `page.tsx`: a phase label, a tension meter (the same scalar that drives the audio), the charge bar, and the two controls. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice and the audio still runs; `AudioContext` created inside the Start pointer handler (autoplay-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2, all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the canonical **EDM build/drop tension-and-release arrangement** (groove ‚Üí riser+filter sweep ‚Üí impact ‚Üí breakdown) formalised as a parametric state machine ¬∑ **Procedural Music Generation** (real-time, state-driven, non-repeating; an exposed "tension knob" steering multiple synthesis layers at once). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß341. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire (three unrelated directions, each dodging every jury-banned tag ‚ÄĒ touch / mic-voice INPUT, inline-SVG OUTPUT, adult-JI-drone VIBE); siblings `386-aeolian-atlas` (live global wind via Open-Meteo ‚Üí **Strouhal aeolian-tone** synthesis ‚Üí Canvas2D wind atlas) + `388-flow-grains` (webcam **optical-flow** field ‚Üí **granular** cloud, AudioFlow / TouchDesigner Optical-Flow-TOP lineage) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß341. **Ambition 4/5**: never-used-technique(#1, EDM build-drop generative arc / club journey engine ‚ÄĒ grep-verified lab-first) + ‚Č•3-subsystems(#2, arc state-machine + multi-layer synth/drum + WebGL2 viz + steering) + named-reference(#3, EDM arrangement canon + PMG tension-knob) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, an alternate journey engine ‚ÄĒ Karel priority #4; deepenable into ritual/jazz/cinematic arc presets + his own *Welcome Home* material as the lead source). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the build‚Üídrop *feels* like real tension/release to actual ears (the whole point, and subjective), scheduler timing jitter under mobile CPU throttling, the limiter's punch/headroom on the DROP transient across browsers, whether the three riser waveform variants read as distinct, and iOS Safari's autoplay idiosyncrasies (no audio/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/387-drop-engine/README.md`

- **[/dream/390-kids-coral-tide](/dream/390-kids-coral-tide)** ‚ÄĒ Coral Tide ūü™ł. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 342.

  **For kids 4+ ‚ÄĒ GROW a singing coral reef by gently shaking the tablet, and HEAR it get bigger as it climbs.** Press the big **"Start the tide ūüĆä"** and shake (or drag a finger, or just watch): glowing plankton drift down through warm amber water and **stick** to a reef that accretes **upward from the seabed** via genuine **Diffusion-Limited Aggregation** (Witten & Sander, *Phys. Rev. Lett.* 47, 1981) with a downward settling bias. The hook is the **audio**: the reef is split into 6 horizontal depth bands and **each band that holds coral sustains one held voice of a D-Dorian chord** (D2 root ‚Üí A2 ‚Üí D3 ‚Üí E3 ‚Üí F3 ‚Üí A3) ‚ÄĒ so as the reef climbs, the chord **STACKS and thickens** from a bare root+fifth into a shimmering Dm11, and every locked branch rings a soft transient bell in its band's pitch. A legible **chord-stack indicator** ("N of 6 voices" + lit pips) makes the growth visible too. **The coral you grow IS the chord, and you can literally hear it get taller.**

  **Why it matters:** this **finally ships the DLA-coral seed the lab banked THREE times** (323/361/383) and never built ‚ÄĒ every prior curation note said "beautiful, but the wow is too gentle." The fix wasn't a new technique, it was a **legible payoff**: harmonic-band-stacking turns a pretty-but-passive blob into something with an instructional read (the jury's explicit #1 ask ‚ÄĒ *feed the legible/instructional lane*, the wins being 358/353/345/365). **DLA is grep-verified lab-first** (no shipped prototype implements it; prior coral seeds were only ever banked). Three subsystems: (1) **`dla.ts`** ‚ÄĒ `CoralSim`: settling-biased Brownian walkers, stick-radius freeze, **spatial-hash** bucket neighbour checks, per-particle depth-band index; `step(intensity)` returns `{locked, activeBands, height01}`; (2) **`audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ `ReefAudio`: a sustained-voice **D-Dorian band-chord engine** (one gain-ramped voice per active band), per-lock triangle bells, always-on faint root, master gain ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast small ears; (3) **`page.tsx`** ‚ÄĒ warm-amber Canvas2D renderer (DPR/resize aware, drifting sunbeams, sandy seabed glow, coral‚Üígold‚Üícream by band height), three input doors + the chord-stack indicator. Degrades cleanly: motion denied/absent ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice + pointer-swish + 3s-idle breathing current (self-grows hands-free within ~2s of Start, so the 06:30 phone reviewer sees+hears it fill in untouched); no Canvas2D ‚Üí readable notice. No API route / no `guard` (Web Audio + Canvas2D, all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

  References: **Witten & Sander, "Diffusion-Limited Aggregation, a Kinetic Critical Phenomenon"** (*Phys. Rev. Lett.* 47, 1981); settling-bias / directional-growth enhancement from **"Toward a Realistic Model of Diffusion-Limited Aggregation: Rotation, Size-Dependent Diffusivities, and Settling"** (ACS Omega). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß342. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach kids fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *a singing DLA coral reef grown by shaking* ‚ÄĒ three technical/aesthetic attacks); siblings `kids-coral-garden` (canonical directional-DLA, immersive teal full-screen) + `kids-reef-glow` (bioluminescent night swarm, size-dependent diffusivity) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß342. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, DLA ‚ÄĒ grep-verified lab-first) + named-reference(#3, Witten & Sander 1981) + recent-research(#5, RESEARCH ¬ß342, this fire). **Diversity**: shake INPUT (fresh, non-banned) ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT (dodges the over-represented WebGL2 output of 375/380/384/387) ¬∑ DLA-with-harmonic-band-stacking TECHNIQUE (lab-first) ¬∑ kids-warm-reef D-Dorian VIBE (warm amber dodges the cool-glow lineage). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the chord thickening *perceptually* reads as "the reef getting bigger" vs. just "more notes" (the central bet ‚ÄĒ voice levels/ramps/bell loudness are first-guess), the shake jerk-threshold + iOS permission flow on real hardware, DLA growth speed/density feel per screen size, and per-frame redraw perf as the particle count grows (no sensor/GPU/audio in this sandbox).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/390-kids-coral-tide/README.md`

- **[/dream/391-resilient-accompanist](/dream/391-resilient-accompanist)** ‚ÄĒ Resilient Accompanist. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 343.

  **The accompanist that survives your mistakes ‚ÄĒ cycle 3 of the Accompanist thread.** Cycle 1 (`375-tempo-canon`) followed your *tempo*; cycle 2 (`380-expressive-accompanist`) added *dynamics + articulation*; this cycle adds the third leg every honest score-following paper says matters most ‚ÄĒ **robustness**. A naive online-DTW follower derails the instant the soloist plays a wrong-note run; this one runs **two followers in parallel and a confidence supervisor that arbitrates between them**. Press nothing ‚ÄĒ a baked "Twinkle Twinkle" (C major, *not* D) auto-plays ~1.5s after load and **deliberately fumbles**: a clean opening ‚Üí a wrong-note run ‚Üí recovery ‚Üí a skip-ahead ‚Üí a hesitation/repeat ‚Üí a clean cadence ‚ÄĒ so a phone reviewer with no MIDI can *hear and see* the recovery hands-free in ~10s. Or play it live on home-row `a s d f g h j k` and fumble on purpose.

  **The lab-first mechanic ‚ÄĒ a dual DTW‚áĄHMM confidence supervisor:** (1) an **online DTW** follower (ported from cycles 1/2, √† la Dixon's MATCH: forward-only, bounded window radius 5, path-slope‚Üítempo) that is smoothest when you're correct and reports an EMA **confidence** that a wrong-note run collapses; (2) an **error-aware HMM** (left-to-right, one state per score note, with explicit `self` / `skip+2,+3` / `back‚ąí1` transitions and a **non-zero emission floor** so a wrong note only *dents* a state rather than annihilating it ‚ÄĒ belief mass survives on the right neighbourhood and the MAP estimate snaps back, after Nakamura et al.); (3) a **hysteresis supervisor** (lo 0.42 / hi 0.70) that hands control to the robust HMM only when DTW confidence truly collapses and back to smooth DTW once it clearly recovers ‚ÄĒ with audible up/down handover blips. The accompaniment plays at the *trusted* follower's position and still carries the full cycle-2 expressive coupling (velocity‚Üígain, IOI-ratio‚Üíenvelope decay). **SVG** viz (the jury banned WebGL2/three.js this cycle): a score timeline with a green **DTW cursor** + dashed-violet **HMM cursor** (the trusted one glows), a faint **belief cloud**, a **confidence band** with threshold guides that visibly plunges on a fumble, and labeled **fumble markers**.

  **Why it matters:** the JURY (2026-06-07) named `380` the lab's only 4/5 and its one real multi-cycle thread, and explicitly told the lab to **extend it instead of opening a fresh explorer**. This is that extension ‚ÄĒ and it stacks the two *expensive* ambition criteria the jury said are nearly absent: **#4 multi-cycle commitment** (cycle 3 of a deliberate thread) + **#5 recent research** (RESEARCH ¬ß343 this fire ‚ÄĒ Matchmaker 2025, Nakamura parallel-HMM, ACCompanion's "Reactivity/Robustness/Expressivity" framing). Also **#1 never-used-technique** (grep-verified: no prior lab prototype runs an HMM or a dual-follower) + **#3 named reference** (Nakamura et al.; Dixon MATCH 2005; ACCompanion IJCAI 2023, arXiv:2304.12939). **Diversity** (vs jury bans): tune in **C major** (dodges the banned D-Dorian/over-used-D center) ¬∑ **SVG** OUTPUT (dodges the banned raw-WebGL2/three.js) ¬∑ dual-follower-supervisor TECHNIQUE (lab-first). The acknowledged input caveat: the thread is inherently keyboard/MIDI (a jury-over-represented input) ‚ÄĒ but the jury *named this exact extension* as the priority, an explicit override of its own input ban for this thread. Three subsystems beyond audio (DTW + HMM + supervisor). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *an accompanist that survives your mistakes* ‚ÄĒ three robustness algorithms); siblings `393-forgiving-accompanist` (particle-filter SMC swarm ‚ÄĒ gorgeous, banked as the cycle-4 candidate) + `392-anticipating-accompanist` (predictive-tempo + delayed-decision anticipation) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß343. No API route / no `guard` (Web Audio + SVG, all client-side; no mic, camera, network, or secrets). Zero new deps. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the DTW‚ÜíHMM handover *perceptually* reads as "it caught my mistake" vs. just a cursor swap, the supervisor threshold/hysteresis feel on a real fumbling human (the lo/hi gates are first-guess), HMM skip/back transition tuning, and live-input latency on real hardware (no audio/keyboard in this sandbox).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/391-resilient-accompanist/README.md`

- **[/dream/423-kids-face-beat](/dream/423-kids-face-beat)** ‚ÄĒ Face Beat ūü•Ā. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 358.

  **For kids 4+ ‚ÄĒ make a BEAT with your FACE.** Press **"Start the face beat ūü•Ā"** and look at the camera: **open your mouth ūüėģ = a kick boom ¬∑ raise your eyebrows ūü§® = a hi-hat tick ¬∑ big smile ūüėĀ = a shaker sparkle ¬∑ puff your cheeks ūüėó = a tom ¬∑ wink ūüėČ = a rim tick.** This is **the lab's first face‚ÜíPERCUSSION mapping** ‚ÄĒ every prior face piece (`258-kids-mirror-pets` face‚Üípentatonic pitch, `413-kids-mouth-mirror`/`393-kids-vowel-color` mic-formant‚Üícolor) mapped expression to *pitch/melody*; this one makes a **drum kit**, so the subject is **RHYTHM, not tuning** ‚ÄĒ no chord, no scale, no melody, pure percussion. It directly answers the **JURY 2026-06-08** two loudest still-open provocations in one fire: **#1** ("build pieces whose subject is rhythm/timbre/noise ‚ÄĒ not tuning ‚Ä¶ the lab fled D-Dorian into a just-intonation monastery") and **#6** ("there is still no MediaPipe face-tracking piece in the window ‚ÄĒ the single biggest untouched first"). The kid-legibility move (mirrors `419-kids-body-band`): every detected hit is **quantized to the nearest 16th-note of a steady 100 BPM groove clock** via a look-ahead scheduler, so even a flailing toddler's random faces lock into a musical beat, and a soft always-on backbone (quiet kick/tom/hat) keeps the groove alive between faces.

  Four subsystems: (1) **`face.ts`** ‚ÄĒ `FaceDetector`: EMA-smooths (őĪ 0.25) the 52 MediaPipe FaceLandmarker blendshape scores, then **rising-edge + per-voice-cooldown (180‚Äď350 ms)** detection across five drum voices (blink threshold raised to 0.55 so normal blinking doesn't fire), plus a `ghostBlendshapes()` 2-bar (32-step) pre-programmed pattern feeding the *same* API; (2) **`audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ pure Web-Audio percussion (kick 150‚Üí45 Hz pitch-env sine + click; hat >8 kHz noise burst; shaker double-bandpass noise; tom 100‚Üí55 Hz; rim bandpassed click) summed through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (‚ąí8 dB / 12:1) ‚Üí master gain so it can never blast small ears, with a **look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms `setInterval`, 100 ms ahead, 16th-grid quantizer ‚ÄĒ Chris Wilson "Two Clocks" pattern); (3) **raw WebGL2** (in `page.tsx`) ‚ÄĒ the mirrored webcam frame as a fullscreen-quad texture + additive radial-Gaussian glow bursts per hit (no Canvas2D, no SVG, no three.js ‚ÄĒ both screen renderers are over-represented this window; WebGL2 = 0√ó in the last-10), seizure-safe soft glows, DPR-aware; (4) HUD/steering in `page.tsx` (emoji legend, privacy notice, tappable ‚Č•72px drum-pad fallback). Degrades cleanly: camera denied/absent ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice + **ghost-face auto-demo within ~2s** (the reviewer always hears a beat hands-free); MediaPipe CDN fails ‚Üí ghost demo + tappable pads; detected-but-still face >4s ‚Üí ghost feeds the detector; no WebGL2 ‚Üí readable notice (audio + pads still work). Camera is **analysis-only** (piped to `detectForVideo()`, never recorded/stored/uploaded; inference runs in-browser WASM). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 + in-browser MediaPipe; the only network is the MediaPipe model/WASM CDN ‚ÄĒ no mic, no recording, no secrets, no FAL_KEY). Zero new npm deps (MediaPipe via `webpackIgnore` CDN `import()`).

  References: **Expotion** (arXiv:2507.04955, 2025) ‚ÄĒ facial-expression + motion control for music generation, the key face‚Üímusic anchor ¬∑ **Ekman & Friesen, Facial Action Coding System (FACS)**, 1978 ‚ÄĒ the scientific basis blendshape naming descends from ¬∑ **MediaPipe FaceLandmarker** (Google, 2023; 52 blendshapes/frame) ¬∑ **Daniel Rozin** soft-mirror lineage (camera-as-instrument) ¬∑ **Chris Wilson**, "A Tale of Two Clocks" (Web-Audio look-ahead scheduling). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß358. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach kids fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *make a BEAT with your face, not a tune* ‚ÄĒ three technical attacks); siblings `424-kids-face-jam` (face‚Üícontinuous **noise/foley texture**, three.js point-cloud ‚ÄĒ the "go weird" timbre take) + `425-kids-face-loop` (face‚Üí**looping step-sequencer with compositional memory**, Canvas2D) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß358. **Ambition 2/5** (stated plainly, no stretch): ‚Č•3-subsystems(#2 ‚ÄĒ detector + quantizing groove scheduler + percussion synth/limiter + WebGL2 render = 4) + named-reference(#3 ‚ÄĒ Expotion 2025 / Ekman-Friesen FACS / Rozin / Wilson). **#1 honestly NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ MediaPipe FaceLandmarker already shipped in `258-kids-mirror-pets`; the *fresh* move is face‚Üí**percussion/rhythm** instead of face‚Üípitch (the same careful framing as `419`'s body‚Üíbeat). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the named anchor (Expotion) is 2025, not <30 days; the dive's honest finding is the *asymmetry* (the 2026 face‚ÜĒaudio front is almost entirely audio‚Üítalking-face, the inverse). **Diversity**: camera/face INPUT (jury-requested #6; mic is banned) ¬∑ **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (the decisive dodge ‚ÄĒ Canvas2D & SVG are the over-represented screen renderers in the last-10; WebGL2 = 0√ó) ¬∑ face-blendshape‚Üíquantized-percussion TECHNIQUE (lab-first mapping) ¬∑ kids silly/percussive **non-tuned** VIBE (the explicit answer to the consonance-resolving rut). Honest flag: camera INPUT now appears in **back-to-back kids ships** (`419` pose + `423` face) ‚ÄĒ but the jury *named* face-tracking the biggest untouched first, taste-authority over the count, and pose‚Č†face (different tracker, different interaction). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the five expressions are *discriminable enough* on a real 4-year-old's face (blink-vs-smile cross-talk; thresholds are first-guess), whether quantized faces *feel* like "I'm playing a beat" vs. lag, the self-flagged builder limitation that **hit-glow bursts use fixed screen-region anchors rather than live landmark X/Y** (next-cycle deepening: pin bursts to real face geometry), iOS camera-permission + AudioContext-in-tap flow, and MediaPipe CDN load time on phone (no camera/GPU/audio in this sandbox).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/423-kids-face-beat/README.md`

- **[/dream/426-euclid-engine](/dream/426-euclid-engine)** ‚ÄĒ Euclid Engine. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 359.

  **An adult, interactive POLYRHYTHM machine ‚ÄĒ pure percussion, ZERO tuning.** Press **Start Engine** and four concentric rings of drums lock into an interlocking groove: each ring's hits are placed by the **Euclidean rhythm algorithm** (Bjorklund ‚ÄĒ spread K onsets as evenly as possible over N steps; E(3,8) = the Afro-Cuban tresillo, E(5,8) = cinquillo, E(7,16) / E(9,16) = jazz/West-African subdivisions), and because each ring runs at `BASE_BPM + a tiny driftBpm`, their downbeats slowly **slip against each other** the way the two pianists in Steve Reich's *Piano Phase* drift apart ‚ÄĒ an interference pattern that never exactly repeats within a listening session, then re-aligns and drifts again. Tap a ring to toggle it, ¬Īits K onsets / N steps, or nudge its phase; the whole thing is a living drum machine you sculpt. It seeds 3‚Äď4 interlocking rings and self-starts ~2s after load so a phone reviewer hears+sees a hypnotic polyrhythm hands-free. **The subject is RHYTHM and TIMBRE, not pitch** ‚ÄĒ no chord, no scale, no melody ‚ÄĒ the **adult** answer to the JURY 2026-06-08 #1 provocation ("build pieces whose subject is rhythm/timbre/noise ‚ÄĒ not tuning"), which until now was answered only for *kids* (`419-kids-body-band` pose‚Üíbeat, `423-kids-face-beat` face‚Üíbeat); there was **no adult rhythm-not-tuning piece**.

  Three subsystems: (1) **Euclidean generator** ‚ÄĒ a Bresenham-form Bjorklund (`onset at step i iff ‚Ćä(i+1)¬∑k/n‚Ćč ‚Č† ‚Ćäi¬∑k/n‚Ćč`, O(n), provably maximally-even for all valid k/n); (2) **phasing scheduler** ‚ÄĒ a Web-Audio **look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms `setInterval`, ~100 ms ahead, Chris Wilson "Two Clocks") where each ring advances on its own slightly-offset clock so the voices gradually phase; synthesized percussion (kick 150‚Üí45 Hz pitch-env sine + click, snare/clap filtered-noise burst, closed-hat short HF noise, clave/rim band-passed click, tom, shaker) through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter ‚Üí master; (3) **three.js render** ‚ÄĒ concentric rotating rings of step-cells (lit = onset), per-ring orbiting playheads, particle/ripple bursts on each hit; perspective camera looking down the stack, DPR-capped, full geometry/material/renderer disposal + rAF/interval/AudioContext teardown on unmount. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL/three.js ‚Üí readable notice + DOM ring readout (audio still runs); AudioContext gesture-gated on mobile ‚Üí prominent "Tap to start". **No API route, no `guard` needed ‚ÄĒ no network at all** (Web Audio + three.js + touch; no mic, no camera, no fetch, no secrets, no FAL_KEY). Zero new npm deps (three.js is an existing repo dep).

  References: **Godfried Toussaint, "The Euclidean Algorithm Generates Traditional Musical Rhythms"** (BRIDGES 2005) ‚ÄĒ the paper that showed Euclidean distributions reproduce traditional world rhythms ¬∑ **Steve Reich**, *Piano Phase* (1967) / *Clapping Music* (1972) ‚ÄĒ the phase-music technique of identical patterns drifting out of sync ¬∑ **Chris Wilson**, "A Tale of Two Clocks" (Web-Audio look-ahead scheduling). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß359.
