Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire (*three ways to leave the consonant monastery* ‚ÄĒ three emotional registers, one per clean renderer lane); siblings `424-welcome-erosion` (Karel's real Welcome Home piano ‚Üí long-form 5-min granular **erosion**, a piece about forgetting ‚ÄĒ Basinski/Lucier ‚ÄĒ Canvas2D) + `425-test-signal` (clinical Ikeda/Alva-Noto **glitch-wall**, raw WebGL2 test-card ‚ÄĒ the purest *refuse-to-resolve* swing) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß359. **Ambition 2/5** (stated plainly): ‚Č•3-subsystems(#2 ‚ÄĒ Euclidean generator + phasing scheduler/percussion synth + three.js render) + named-reference(#3 ‚ÄĒ Toussaint 2005 / Reich / Wilson). **Diversity**: touch INPUT (mic + camera both rested/banned) ¬∑ **three.js OUTPUT** (the decisive dodge ‚ÄĒ 0√ó in the last-10; the freshest clean renderer lane, where WebGL2/Canvas2D/SVG are all recently used or jury-banned) ¬∑ Euclidean-rhythm + Reich-phasing TECHNIQUE ¬∑ hypnotic-percussive **zero-tuning** VIBE (the adult answer to the consonance-resolving rut). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the per-ring phase-drift *perceptually* reads as "the rings are slipping against each other" vs. just polyrhythm at a phone glance; the builder-flagged `setTimeout`-per-tick playhead churn on a throttled/backgrounded tab (harmless but a possible glitch source on low-end mobile); iOS AudioContext-in-tap + autostart path; three.js perf/DPR on a low-end phone; whether synthesized kick/snare/hat/clave read as a satisfying kit on phone speakers (no audio/GPU in this sandbox).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/426-euclid-engine/README.md`

- **[/dream/429-kids-texture-paint-tap](/dream/429-kids-texture-paint-tap)** ‚ÄĒ Texture Paint ūüé®. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 360.

  **For kids 4+ ‚ÄĒ finger-paint where each brush *sounds like what it looks like*, and the sound is pure TEXTURE, never a note.** Pick a brush by tapping a big emoji swatch ‚ÄĒ **ūüĆŅ Crunch ¬∑ ūüęß Pop ¬∑ ūü™Ķ Tap ¬∑ ‚ö° Scratch ¬∑ ūüíß Splash** ‚ÄĒ then dab or drag on the dark canvas: each touch stamps a matching textured mark AND fires a matching procedural-**foley** sound in the same frame. This is **the lab's first pure-NOISE/foley kids instrument played by touch** ‚ÄĒ every prior kids sound toy (and the loved paint lineage `100`‚Ě§ÔłŹ/`104`‚Ě§ÔłŹ/`158`‚Ě§ÔłŹ/`160`‚Ě§ÔłŹ/`152`‚Ě§ÔłŹ/`223`‚Ě§ÔłŹ, and the weather toys `293`/`115`/`306`) maps to **pentatonic PITCH**; this one has **zero notes, zero scale, zero melody** ‚ÄĒ the entire palette is shaped noise & timbre. It is the **adult-jury's #1 mandate** ("build pieces whose subject is rhythm/timbre/**NOISE** ‚ÄĒ not tuning") finally answered for **kids**, via a **non-camera, non-mic** route (the prior kids rhythm pieces `419`/`423` were both camera ‚ÄĒ this rests the camera and uses touch).

  Three subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **`audio.ts`** ‚ÄĒ an **impulse + resonator foley engine**: five voices, each a short noise excitation (white burst / click / friction sweep) through **randomized inharmonic bandpass/highpass resonators** so a material character emerges but **no pitch ever does** (centre frequencies are re-randomized across wide inharmonic ranges every hit, so repeated dabs never spell a tune) ‚ÄĒ Crunch = a cluster of 4‚Äď8 gravel crackles, Pop = a rounded band-filtered blip (bouba), Tap = a click through two offset damped resonances (dry knock), Scratch = a highpassed friction rasp (kiki), Splash = a burst + descending-LP drip tail; **per-voice cooldowns** (45‚Äď90 ms) stop rapid drags machine-gunning, everything sums through a **`DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter** (‚ąí6 dB / 20:1) at master 0.22 so it can never blast small ears; a very quiet always-on **airy noise bed** (bandpassed, non-pitched) means it's never silent. (2) **`scene.ts`** ‚ÄĒ a **Canvas2D** textured-stamp renderer: five `draw*` functions (spiky star-clusters for Crunch, soft radial bubbles for Pop, angular wood grain for Tap, jagged scratch radiations, radial drip splatter) with a seeded LCG for organic per-stamp variation. (3) **input/auto-demo** in `page.tsx` ‚ÄĒ multi-touch via Pointer Events + `setPointerCapture`, a hands-free **auto-demo** at 1.8 s that dabs/drags through all five brushes (so a 06:30 phone reviewer sees+hears it with no interaction), a **tap-to-start** overlay creating/resuming the `AudioContext` inside the gesture (iOS-safe), resize-preserving canvas, full teardown on unmount. Degrades cleanly (no Canvas ‚Üí notice; autoplay blocked ‚Üí tap prompt). Camera/mic: **none**. No API route, no `guard` needed ‚ÄĒ **no network at all** (no mic, camera, fetch, secrets, FAL_KEY). Zero new npm deps.

  References: **Wolfgang K√∂hler** ‚ÄĒ bouba/kiki cross-modal sound-symbolism (1929; round shapes ‚ÜĒ soft sounds, spiky ‚ÜĒ sharp ‚ÄĒ the toy's mapping principle) ¬∑ **Andy Farnell**, *Designing Sound* (2010) ‚ÄĒ procedural-foley lineage ¬∑ **Perry Cook** ‚ÄĒ PhISM / physically-informed particle-foley. Born from RESEARCH ¬ß360. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach kids fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *paint where the brush's texture IS its sound, noise not notes* ‚ÄĒ three synthesis attacks); siblings `428-kids-texture-paint-morph` (subtractive noise-morph on a **raw WebGL2** painterly ping-pong-FBO canvas ‚ÄĒ the most beautiful take, the cycle-2 visual deepening) + `427-kids-texture-paint-grains` (granular-noise foley, Canvas2D) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß360. **Ambition 2/5** (stated plainly, no stretch): ‚Č•3-subsystems(#2 ‚ÄĒ foley synth engine + ambient bed + Canvas2D stamp renderer + pointer/auto-demo = 4) + named-reference(#3 ‚ÄĒ K√∂hler 1929 ¬∑ Farnell 2010 ¬∑ Cook PhISM). **#1 honestly NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ noise/granular/modal synth all exist in the lab; the fresh move is the **all-noise-no-pitch framing + texture‚ÜĒtimbre cross-modal mapping for kids**. **Diversity**: **touch INPUT** (the clean dodge of the over-represented/jury-banned mic; camera rested after back-to-back `419`/`423`) ¬∑ **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (3√ó in last-10, allowed) ¬∑ impulse-resonator **NOISE-foley TECHNIQUE** ¬∑ kids non-tuned texture VIBE (the literal answer to the consonance rut). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the five voices read as distinctly "crunchy/poppy/scratchy" on phone speakers (resonator constants are first-guess), iOS AudioContext-resume-in-gesture + autoplay-blocked tap-prompt path, the self-flagged `alert()`-based design-notes link (functional but inelegant on mobile; left to avoid risking the build), and Canvas2D stamp + rapid multi-touch perf on a low-end tablet.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/429-kids-texture-paint-tap/README.md`

- **[/dream/437-wiki-pulse](/dream/437-wiki-pulse)** ‚ÄĒ Wiki-Pulse. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 363.

  **What does the entire planet's live editing of human knowledge SOUND like ‚ÄĒ right now, this second?** Tap once and the piece connects to the **Wikimedia EventStreams `recentchange` SSE firehose** (`stream.wikimedia.org/v2/stream/recentchange`) ‚ÄĒ a public, key-less, CORS-enabled Server-Sent-Events stream of *every edit to every Wikipedia/Wikidata/Commons in real time* ‚ÄĒ and sonifies it live: every glowing point in the 3D field is a real edit happening as you watch. This is **the lab's first sonification of a live human-activity stream** (grep-verified: prior data pieces are planetary/physical ‚ÄĒ `418-seismic-pulse`, `314-solar-wind`, `262-aurora-particle`, `233-earth-pulse` ‚ÄĒ never a *human* tide), resurrecting the banked `430-wiki-pulse` (cycle 361, never built). It answers **two** JURY 2026-06-08 provocations in one fire: **#4** ("real-world data sonification ‚ÄĒ music about something other than music ‚ÄĒ zero in 15 cycles") and **#2** ("build something that *refuses to resolve* ‚ÄĒ there is no Ikeda/Akten counter-piece") ‚ÄĒ an aperiodic firehose with no grid, no arc, no chord, that structurally cannot resolve.

  **The sonification (the bot/human tide is the idea):** five distinct voices through an 8-voice cap ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` limiter (‚ąí8 dB) ‚Üí master. **Bot edit = a dry, cold, high bandpassed-noise click** (you literally *hear* the machine layer under the human one); **human main-article edit = a warm triangle pluck with glide** ‚ÄĒ *addition* glides up (Hatnote "bell") / *removal* falls and softens (Hatnote "string"), magnitude ‚Üí register (small = high+quiet, big = low+loud); **new page = a brief two-osc stab**; **log = a low sine thud**; **categorize = a quiet ultra-high tick**. Pitches are equal-tempered (the JURY banned just-intonation-as-subject this cycle) and never voiced as a chord ‚ÄĒ arrivals are aperiodic, so it never resolves. **Visuals (three.js):** a 600-slot circular-buffer point field with a custom GLSL `ShaderMaterial` (per-point alpha, additive bloom) ‚ÄĒ **X = wiki/language** (enwiki left ‚Üí wikidata right), **Y = byte delta** (additions above, removals below), size ‚ąĚ log|őĒbytes|, color = cyan(human)/amber(bot)/magenta(new)/grey-green(log), ~3‚Äď4 s lifetime; gentle camera drift; full geometry/material/renderer + rAF disposal on unmount. Clinical glowing-point-on-black aesthetic (Ikeda *data-cosm* lineage). Live HUD: edits/sec, % bot, total, and an honest **live/demo status line**.

  **Graceful degradation (always demos hands-free):** if the SSE fails to deliver a message within 4 s (network block / CORS), a **synthetic generator** emits realistic fake `recentchange`-shaped events (weighted wiki distribution, ~12 % bot, realistic byte-delta tail, ~10/sec) through the *same* sonify+visualize path, with the status line honestly switching to `text-amber-300` "demo stream (live feed unavailable)"; if it was live and later errors, it bridges on demo and retries. Tap-to-start creates/resumes the `AudioContext` inside the gesture (iOS-safe) and kicks the connection. **No API route, no `guard` needed** ‚ÄĒ the SSE is consumed *client-side* by the browser's native `EventSource` directly from Wikimedia; there is no Next.js route, no mic, no camera, no secrets, no FAL_KEY. Zero new npm deps (`three` is an existing repo dep).

  References: **Hatnote, *Listen to Wikipedia*** (Stephen LaPorte & Mahmoud Hashemi, 2013 ‚ÄĒ bells for additions, strings for removals, pitch ‚ąĚ edit size; the canonical prior art, honored and made colder/data-clinical here) ¬∑ **Ryoji Ikeda, *data-cosm [n¬į1]*** (180 Studios, on view through Feb 2026 ‚ÄĒ the clinical glowing-data-on-black visual language). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß363. **Ambition 3/5** (honest): **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (live SSE ingest/fallback controller + 5-voice sonification engine/limiter + three.js GLSL point-field) + **#3** named references (Hatnote / Ikeda) + **#5** recent research (RESEARCH ¬ß363, this fire, <14 days). **#1 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ data sonification and three.js both exist in the lab; the *fresh* move is the **live human-activity stream** + the audible bot/human timbral split. **Diversity** (vs JURY bans): **live-SSE/API INPUT** (dodges the banned mic and the over-represented touch) ¬∑ **three.js OUTPUT** (dodges the banned SVG) ¬∑ **data-stream-sonification TECHNIQUE** (dodges the banned Kuramoto) ¬∑ **clinical refuse-to-resolve VIBE** (the literal answer to the consonant-resolving rut; equal-tempered, not just-intonation). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire (*three ways to refuse to resolve, via three distinct inputs*); siblings `436-flow-static` (camera optical-flow ‚Üí Ikeda data-noise, raw WebGL2) + `438-no-input` (no-input feedback-delay-network self-oscillator, Canvas2D scope) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß363. **Build-verified (exit 0), not browser-verified** ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the live feed actually connects from Karel's network (the synthetic fallback covers it either way), whether ~10 evts/sec sounds comfortable vs. cluttered on a phone speaker, whether 8 concurrent voices balance, and the three.js additive-bloom appearance on OLED vs LCD (no network/audio/GPU in this sandbox).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/437-wiki-pulse/README.md`

- **[/dream/440-kids-comet-gather](/dream/440-kids-comet-gather)** ‚ÄĒ Comet Gather. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 364.

  **For kids 4+ ‚ÄĒ hold your phone up like a window and physically turn/sweep it to look around a 3D night sky, scooping up drifting glowing motes into your own growing constellation ‚ÄĒ and every mote you gather adds a voice to a slowly evolving lullaby that is different at minute 5 than at minute 0.** No tapping, no reading: the instrument is the child *moving the phone through space*. A soft purple aura reticle sits at screen center; hold it over a drifting mote for a moment and it is scooped with a bell chime, flies to join a slowly-rotating cluster connected by faint glowing lines, and starts singing. This is the lab's **first gyro "magic-window" first-person kids piece** (all ~115 prior kids prototypes are touch/2D or tilt-to-roll `238`; this one is a 3D look-around portal) **and its first long-form *evolving* generative kids lullaby** (a piece with state/memory that transforms over minutes ‚ÄĒ the categorical-diversity-menu's long-form-generative slot, unfilled for kids). It deliberately **rests the over-represented touch input** (touch was 4√ó in the last-10 ‚Üí diversity-banned this cycle) and the **camera-body kids lane** (`419`/`423`/`234`), using **device-orientation** instead ‚ÄĒ embodied/sensorimotor, KIDS.md's core principle, and it gets the child physically turning to look around.

  Three+ subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **gyro magic-window camera** ‚ÄĒ `deviceorientation` őĪ/ő≤/ő≥ ‚Üí yaw/pitch, low-pass-smoothed (őĪ=0.12) onto the three.js camera (iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` called inside the Begin tap; drag-to-look fallback on desktop/no-sensor with a readable note); (2) **three.js 3D scene** ‚ÄĒ ~30 Fibonacci-distributed drifting glowing motes (AdditiveBlending sprites) + ~1600-point background starfield surrounding the viewer on a sphere, constellation fly-to + slow-orbit cluster, glowing connector lines, full geometry/material/renderer disposal on unmount; (3) **long-form generative audio engine** ‚ÄĒ D-major-pentatonic (no wrong notes), each gathered mote a sustained/arpeggiated triangle+harmonic voice through a **`StereoPannerNode`** (spatial L/R by screen position ‚ÄĒ chosen over HRTF so it reads on phone speakers) ‚Üí **`DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter** (safe for small ears); the evolution is real: the pad's root/fifth drift D2‚ÜíE2‚ÜíA2 on an ~8-min cycle, a ~4-min filter LFO breathes the texture, each voice's arpeggio rate grows with age + global time (busier/richer by minute 5), and a ~12-min "goodnight" fade for bedtime; (4) **input/auto-demo** ‚ÄĒ a hands-free auto-demo sweeps the camera in a figure-8 and auto-gathers the first ~4 motes so the lullaby self-builds within ~20s on a 06:30 phone open with no interaction; auto-pan resumes after idle. **No network at all** (no mic, camera, fetch, secrets, FAL_KEY); **no API route, no `guard` needed.** Zero new npm deps (`three` is an existing repo dep).

  References: **"magic window" gyro-stargazer interaction** ‚ÄĒ the 3-DoF device-orientation camera without a headset (Google Cardboard-era WebVR demos; W3C `DeviceOrientation` / Gyroscope spec, Candidate Recommendation Draft 2026-02-02) ¬∑ **Brian Eno generative-music lineage** (*Discreet Music* / *Music for Airports* ‚ÄĒ a system that sounds different at minute 1, 5, 12) ¬∑ **3-DoF head-tracked spatial audio** via `StereoPannerNode` (Web Audio, no HRTF ‚ÄĒ phone-speaker-friendly). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß364. **Ambition 2/5** (honest, no stretch): **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (gyro camera + three.js scene + long-form spatial-audio engine + auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named references (magic-window stargazer / Eno generative / 3-DoF spatial audio). **#1 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ gyro (`238`/`417`/`83`), 3D (`238`), and stereo-pan spatial audio (`99`/`238`) all exist in the lab; the *fresh* moves are the **first-person look-around magic window** + the **long-form evolving lullaby** for kids. **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the dive's hard anchor (W3C Gyroscope CR, Feb 2026) is ~4 months old, not <14 days. **Diversity** (vs cycle bans): **device-orientation INPUT** (the clean dodge of the count-banned touch [4√ó] and the jury-banned mic) ¬∑ **three.js OUTPUT** (2√ó in last-10, allowed) ¬∑ **magic-window + long-form-generative TECHNIQUE** ¬∑ **kids-cosmic VIBE** (parity-exempt). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach kids fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *turn the phone to look around a 3D singing night* ‚ÄĒ three technical attacks; alternating off three consecutive WIDE cycles 361/362/363); siblings `438-kids-star-window` (three.js *aim-to-wake* a fixed star-creature ‚ÄĒ the most immediate/kid-legible take, quaternion-slerp gyro) + `439-kids-star-night` (the same aim-to-wake on a **raw-WebGL2** GLSL additive-glow point-field ‚ÄĒ the atmospheric renderer attack) build-reviewed (tsc + ESLint clean, ~519/495 lines) and banked in IDEAS ¬ß364. **Build-verified (exit 0, prerendered, zero warnings from the folder), NOT browser-verified** (no gyro/audio/GPU in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the 0.9s scoop dwell feels right vs. fiddly for a 4-year-old holding a moving phone on a drifting target, the iOS `requestPermission` + őĪ/ő≤/ő≥‚Üíyaw/pitch frame on real iOS/Android hardware (axis quirks across OEMs), whether the multi-minute root-drift/goodnight evolution reads at full length, `StereoPanner` on mobile Safari, and three.js AdditiveBlending bloom across mobile GPUs.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/440-kids-comet-gather/README.md`

- **[/dream/441-latent-listening-room](/dream/441-latent-listening-room)** ‚ÄĒ Latent Listening Room. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 365.

  **A generative ambient piece that continuously *dreams a picture of itself* ‚ÄĒ and the picture bends the music back.** Press **Begin** and a slowly-evolving pad+arpeggio starts (fully synthesized, expressive 12-TET, an 8-chord progression cycling every 15‚Äď25s). An `AnalyserNode` reads the live spectrum every frame and, every ~6 seconds, writes a text prompt from it ‚ÄĒ **brightness** (spectral centroid) ‚Üí palette words (deep indigo ‚ÜĒ luminous gold), **energy/RMS** ‚Üí density words (ethereal mist ‚ÜĒ swirling turbulence), **dominant pitch-class** ‚Üí a hue accent ‚ÄĒ over a fixed base style (*"abstract volumetric light, latent dreamscape, soft caustics, Refik-Anadol-like data-pigment, cinematic"*). The prompt POSTs to a **guarded** route that calls FAL `flux/schnell` (4 steps), and when the image returns it **cross-fades** over the previous one on a full-bleed canvas with a slow **Ken-Burns** drift, so the picture is always alive. Then the loop closes: the new image is sampled to an 8√ó6 offscreen canvas, its average color read, and the audio bent ‚ÄĒ **brighter image ‚Üí opens the master lowpass + adds a shimmer layer; warmer hue ‚Üí longer reverb tail**. Audio ‚Üí prompt ‚Üí image ‚Üí audio. This is the lab's **first *closed* audio‚ÜĒimage‚ÜĒaudio loop** and the freshest hit on Karel's standing directive ("AI image generation INSIDE audio-visual experiments, where the image responds to or shapes the audio ‚ÄĒ the path that interests him most right now"); it rides the loved `323-latent-condensation`‚Ě§ÔłŹ latent-pigment aesthetic.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **Web-Audio generative synth + FFT analysis** ‚ÄĒ detuned saw/triangle/sine pad stacks with cross-fades on chord change, plucked-sine arpeggio, convolver reverb, shimmer highpass, slow LFO filter mod, everything through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter ‚Üí master; FFT 2048 ‚Üí energy (8 log bands) / centroid / chromagram pitch-class. (2) **Guarded FAL image pipeline** ‚ÄĒ `api/route.ts` starts POST with `const blocked = await guard(req); if (blocked) return blocked;` (origin + 8/min + 40/day), returns 501 when `FAL_KEY` is absent, else `fal.subscribe("fal-ai/flux/schnell")`; client paces ‚Č§1 request in flight, ‚Č•7s apart; images preloaded with `crossOrigin="anonymous"`. (3) **Canvas compositor** ‚ÄĒ cross-fade (~2.5s) + Ken-Burns + vignette. (4) **Image‚Üíaudio loop-back** ‚ÄĒ `getImageData` average-color ‚Üí `linearRampToValueAtTime` on lowpass cutoff / shimmer gain / reverb wet (CORS-taint `try/catch` ‚Üí degrades to image-only, never crashes). **Graceful degradation (the actual in-build experience, since the sandbox has no FAL_KEY):** any failure (501 / 429 / network / load / taint) falls to a **locally-synthesized field** driven by the *same* spectral data ‚ÄĒ per-pixel sum-of-sines plasma, 3 orbiting radial-gradient blobs in pitch-class hues, ~220 curl-flow-field particles, Ken-Burns + vignette ‚ÄĒ with an honest status line (emerald `text-emerald-300/95` "dreaming live" when images arrive, amber `text-amber-300/95` "live image unavailable ‚ÄĒ showing synthesized field" in fallback). It is a complete, beautiful AV piece even with no key, and upgrades live when one is present.

  References: **Refik Anadol** ‚ÄĒ *Unsupervised* (MoMA, 2022) / *Machine Hallucinations* (latent-space pigment flows; the explicit visual target) ¬∑ **Memo Akten** ‚ÄĒ *Learning to See* (2017/2019; a network continuously re-describing live input ‚ÄĒ the conceptual ancestor of the audio‚Üíimage‚Üíaudio feedback) ¬∑ **arXiv 2604.07612** (Apr 2026, *Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄďAI Musical Co-Performance: Accompaniment Generation with Latent Diffusion Models and MAX/MSP* ‚ÄĒ the audio‚ÜĒlatent feedback framing, realized here browser-natively via a hosted fast model rather than a local diffusion model; cited as research context, ~2mo, not a <14d anchor). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß365. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire (*three frontier-different adult directions*); siblings `442-body-orchestra` (adult full-body MediaPipe spatial conductor's room ‚ÄĒ jury #6's "single biggest untouched first") + `443-the-vanishing` (a 10-voice ensemble that permanently loses a voice every time you look away ‚ÄĒ attention-as-instrument, the jury's "go weird") build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß365. **Ambition 2/5** (honest): **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (synth+FFT ¬∑ guarded FAL pipeline ¬∑ canvas compositor ¬∑ image‚Üíaudio loop-back) + **#3** named references (Anadol / Akten / 2604.07612). **#1 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ image-gen already exists in the lab (271/153/323); the fresh move is the *closed* audio‚ÜĒimage‚ÜĒaudio loop + spectral‚Üíprompt mapping (application/framing novelty, not a new primitive). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the dive anchor is ~2 months, not <14 days. **Diversity** (vs cycle bans): **synthesized-audio/none INPUT** (dodges banned mic + over-leaned touch) ¬∑ **AI-IMAGE OUTPUT** (0√ó in last-10 ‚ÄĒ a genuinely fresh modality; dodges banned SVG + over-leaned three.js) ¬∑ **generative-model-call + spectral-conditioning + audio-loop-back TECHNIQUE** (dodges banned Kuramoto) ¬∑ **latent / Anadol-organic VIBE** (not JI-tuning-as-subject). **Build-verified (exit 0, prerendered, zero warnings from the folder; the guarded `/api` route is in the manifest), NOT browser-verified** (no FAL_KEY / no GPU / no audio in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the live FAL path connects from Karel's network + the FAL CDN's CORS policy for the `getImageData` loop-back (try/catch-guarded ‚Üí image-only if tainted), whether ~1 image/7s reads as "breathing with the music," whether the synthesized-field fallback holds as a standalone piece on a phone, and the iOS AudioContext-in-tap path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/441-latent-listening-room/README.md`

- **[/dream/454-piano-caption-loom](/dream/454-piano-caption-loom)** ‚ÄĒ Piano Caption Loom. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 369.

  **Cycle 2 of the "Latent Piano Room" spine ‚ÄĒ the caption a latent image is dreamed from is no longer written in one shot; you watch a council of agents critique and *refine* it across visible rounds, from Karel's real recorded piano.** Press **Play Karel's Piano** and his Ghost recording streams through a Web-Audio chain (or drop a *Welcome Home* track; or a warm C‚ÄďAm‚ÄďF‚ÄďG7‚ÄďCmaj9 synth fallback that **resolves on purpose** if the recording can't load ‚ÄĒ never silent). A musically-aware analyser (FFT 2048) tracks RMS dynamics, a 12-bin chromagram ‚Üí dominant pitch-class + major/minor/consonance, spectral-flux onsets, phrase boundaries, and a smoothed **valence** (modality+consonance) √ó **arousal** (dynamics+onset-density) ‚ÄĒ the music's emotion target. At every phrase boundary / onset cluster the **Caption Loom** runs a 3-round refinement: **Round 0 ‚ÄĒ Draft** (four specialist proposers ‚ÄĒ Scene/Palette/Motion/Style ‚ÄĒ each emit a clause from the music); **Critic** scores the draft against the emotion target and writes concrete critiques ("palette too cool for this warm phrase", "motion too calm for high arousal"); **Revise** ‚ÄĒ proposers rewrite the flagged clauses; **Final** anchors the emotion tag. The HUD stacks all three rounds ‚ÄĒ round labels, **rising confidence bars**, the critic's amber notes, and the specific **changed words highlighted in violet** ‚ÄĒ so you watch the prompt *sharpen*. The final caption POSTs to a **guarded** route ‚Üí FAL `flux/schnell`; the returning image cross-fades as the backdrop and its average color **bends the real piano back** (brightness ‚Üí master lowpass + shimmer; warmth ‚Üí reverb tail). The loop: real piano ‚Üí analysis ‚Üí emotion-aligned iterative caption ‚Üí image ‚Üí piano.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 6): (1) **real-recorded-audio chain** ‚ÄĒ `<audio crossOrigin="anonymous">` default `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶` (Ghost recording) / file-drop / warm resolving synth fallback ‚Üí `createMediaElementSource` ‚Üí AnalyserNode ‚Üí reverb‚Üícompressor‚Üímaster, with a 350ms all-zero CORS-taint check that auto-swaps to the synth. (2) **musically-aware analysis** (`analysis.ts`) ‚ÄĒ chromagram/modality/consonance + spectral-flux onset (Bello 2005) + phrase-boundary + valence-arousal. (3) **iterative multi-agent caption loom** (`loom.ts`) ‚ÄĒ propose‚Üícritique‚Üírevise‚Üífinal, deterministic (no LLM calls), all rounds recorded for the HUD. (4) **guarded FAL image pipeline** ‚ÄĒ `api/route.ts` starts POST with `const blocked = await guard(req); if (blocked) return blocked;`, 501 when `FAL_KEY` absent. (5) **synthesized latent field** (`field.ts`) ‚ÄĒ plasma/particles driven by the same caption + features so the piece is complete with no key (amber "synthesized (no image key)" badge; the loom still runs all 3 rounds). (6) **image‚Üíaudio loopback** ‚ÄĒ `getImageData` avg color (try/catch CORS taint ‚Üí image-only) ‚Üí ramps on lowpass/shimmer/reverb on the live playback. iOS-safe (`AudioContext.resume()` inside the start tap). `@fal-ai/client` is an existing repo dep ‚ÄĒ **no new npm deps**.

  References: **arXiv 2507.20536** (*T2I-Copilot*) + **arXiv 2511.11483** (*ImAgent*) ‚ÄĒ training-free multi-agent **propose‚Üícritique‚Üírefine** prompt loops (the loom's core mechanic; RESEARCH ¬ß369) ¬∑ **arXiv 2512.23320** (*Multi-Agents Semantic Emotion Aligned Music to Image*, Dec 2025) ‚ÄĒ music-derived captions + **valence-arousal** emotion alignment ¬∑ **Russell (1980)** circumplex model of affect ¬∑ **Refik Anadol** *Machine Hallucinations* / **Memo Akten** *Learning to See* (latent-pigment aesthetic) ¬∑ **Bello et al. (2005)** spectral-flux onset detection. **Ambition 4/5** ‚ÄĒ directly answering the jury's "zero pieces at 4‚Äď5/5" regression: **#1** never-used technique (the lab's **first iterative self-critiquing prompt-refinement** subsystem ‚ÄĒ a Critic agent that scores a draft and triggers a revise loop; prior "multi-agent" in `327-physarum-choir` is an agent-*based particle sim*, a different sense) + **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (6, above) + **#3** named references (T2I-Copilot/ImAgent/2512.23320/Russell/Anadol/Akten/Bello) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 2 of the jury-mandated Latent Piano Room spine, the #4 axis the jury said died at 414). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the dive anchors are ~3‚Äď11 months, not <14 days. **Diversity** (jury 2026-06-09 bans Canvas2D-OUTPUT ¬∑ drum-machine-TECH ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE ¬∑ calm-cosmic-pentatonic-kids): **real-recorded-audio INPUT** (sanctioned spine continuation) ¬∑ **AI-image OUTPUT** (the canvas only composites the AI image + HUD, not Canvas2D-as-the-piece ‚ÄĒ the same dodge as `441`/`448`, and the jury named this lineage the bright spot to extend) ¬∑ **iterative-caption-refinement TECHNIQUE** (not drum-machine) ¬∑ **warm/expressive/resolves-on-purpose VIBE** (the jury's explicit "missing middle"). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *a multi-agent caption pipeline composes the dream from the real piano* ‚ÄĒ three attacks); siblings `452-piano-agent-council` (the visible one-shot council + Russell dial ‚ÄĒ most legible, the cycle-3 VIZ candidate) + `453-piano-affect-atlas` (valence-arousal comet cartography) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß369. **Build-verified (exit 0, `‚úď Compiled successfully in 111s`, prerendered `/dream/454-piano-caption-loom 13.6 kB` + guarded `/api` in the manifest, `npx eslint` on the folder exit 0 / zero warnings), NOT browser-verified** (no FAL_KEY / no audio / no GPU in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶` resolves + Supabase signed-URL CORS lets the analyser read non-zero from Karel's network (all-zero‚Üísynth fallback covers it); whether the critic's critiques *fire* and the caption visibly improves on real expressive piano (vs. trivially passing on round 0); whether ~1 image/phrase reads as "painting the music"; the FAL CDN's CORS for the loopback `getImageData` (try/catch‚Üíimage-only if tainted); iOS AudioContext-in-tap.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/454-piano-caption-loom/README.md`

- **[/dream/460-kids-ball-pit](/dream/460-kids-ball-pit)** ‚ÄĒ Ball Pit. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 372. **For**: kids (4+).

  **The lab's FIRST many-body collision physics ‚ÄĒ tip the phone and pour ~900 glowing marbles that pile, slosh, and chime as they collide.** Gravity follows device tilt (gamma/beta), with a pointer-drag fallback when DeviceOrientation is denied/absent. Every ball-ball or ball-wall collision whose impact speed exceeds 60 px/s rings a soft bell pitched to a **D-major just-intonation hexachord** (D E F‚ôĮ A B, ratios 1 : 9/8 : 5/4 : 3/2 : 5/3, two octaves), the pitch chosen by the ball's hue ‚ÄĒ so a cascade tumbling into a corner becomes a sparkling arpeggio and a gentle slosh is a few soft chimes. Tap to pour ~24 fresh balls (a little waterfall of 7 chimes); two-finger fast swipe scrambles the pile into a glittery sparkle-run. The instrument is gravity; the child is the composer; the sound comes from the collision, not a sequencer.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **uniform-grid spatial-hash collision solver** (`physics.ts`) ‚ÄĒ broad-phase buckets every ball by `floor(x/cell), floor(y/cell)` with cell = 2.2√ó max radius (O(n¬≤)‚Üí~O(n)); narrow-phase computes penetration `minDist ‚ąí actualDist`, applies a mass-split positional correction (PBD constraint) + a restitution velocity impulse along the collision normal; stable `id`-packed pair-dedup (`lo*1048576+hi`, no `indexOf`). (2) **WebGL2 instanced-quad renderer** ‚ÄĒ one `drawArraysInstanced` per frame for all ~900‚Äď1400 balls; each instance carries position/radius/hue/flash; the fragment shader draws core + glow ring from `length(vUV)` with additive premultiplied-alpha blending over a deep `#090a12` ground. (3) **JI bell synth** (`audio.ts`) ‚ÄĒ sine fundamental + inharmonic partial √ó2.756 + 12 ms band-passed noise burst, 8 ms attack / ~1.1 s exp decay; soft D3‚ÄďF‚ôĮ3‚ÄďA3 ambient pad (‚Čą‚ąí35 dBFS, LFO tremolo) so it's **never silent**; everything through a DynamicsCompressor (‚ąí18 dB, 4:1) ‚Üí brick-wall limiter (‚ąí2 dB, 20:1) so no transient can pierce; an 8-events-per-step cap prevents chime-spam at high ball counts. (4) **tilt / pointer / tap input** ‚ÄĒ first-tap boots AudioContext + requests iOS DeviceOrientation permission; gravity from tilt or drag; tap-to-pour; two-finger scramble. **4-second hands-free auto-demo** gently rocks gravity so the pit self-demos at a 06:30 glance.

  References: **M√ľller, Heidelberger, Hennix & Ratcliff (2007)** *Position Based Dynamics* (the positional-correction constraint + complementary velocity update for non-penetration) ¬∑ **"Party: A WebGPU Particle Physics Playground"** (webgpu.com, **Jan 23 2026** ‚ÄĒ thousands of GPU-collided particles as a musical instrument; the "collision-as-instrument" framing; RESEARCH ¬ß372). This prototype uses CPU spatial-hash physics + WebGL2 rendering rather than WebGPU for broad mobile compatibility. **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (the lab's **first many-body collision / contact solver** ‚ÄĒ grep-verified clean at README level across 360+ prototypes; the lab has fluid `3`/`84`‚Ě§ÔłŹ/`433`, particle-life `8`/`16`/`236`, falling-sand `332`, ferrofluid `456`, soft-body `451`, but no contact solver) + **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (4, above) + **#3** named references (M√ľller et al. PBD 2007 ¬∑ Party Jan 2026). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the Party anchor is ~4.5 months, not <14 days. **Diversity** (cycle bans: AI-image OUT [4√ó count] ¬∑ Canvas2D OUT [jury] ¬∑ drum-machine TECH [jury] ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve VIBE [jury] ¬∑ calm-pentatonic-lullaby kids template [jury]): **tilt/device-orientation INPUT** (the embodied dodge of touch) ¬∑ **raw-WebGL2 instanced OUTPUT** (zero Canvas2D anywhere ‚ÄĒ dodges both the jury Canvas2D ban and the AI-image count-ban) ¬∑ **spatial-hash COLLISION/contact-solver TECHNIQUE** (not drum-machine) ¬∑ **loud-playful-physical VIBE** (a glittery cascade ‚ÄĒ not refuse-to-resolve, not the pentatonic-lullaby template). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire (three unrelated fresh registers); siblings `461-kids-storm-conductor` (touch ‚Üí WebGL2 fractal-lightning that sings, random-midpoint-displacement bolts + per-tip JI bells) + `462-kids-gear-garden` (spin a gear-train where integer tooth-ratios ARE just-intonation intervals ‚ÄĒ Partch/Antikythera) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß372. Rides loved `169-kids-marble-run`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (physics-toy) + `83-kids-tilt-rain`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (tilt) + `84-wave-fluid`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (slosh) + `130-tsl-particle-compute`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (glowing particles). **Build-verified (exit 0, prerendered `/dream/460-kids-ball-pit/page.js` in the manifest, `npx eslint` on the folder exit 0 / zero warnings), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU / sensor / audio in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether ~900‚Äď1400 balls hold 60 fps on a real low-end phone (unprofiled); whether the collision-chime cascade reads as a sparkling arpeggio vs. spam (the 8-events/step cap guards it); whether tilt-steering feels good without quaternion correction; the iOS DeviceOrientation-permission + AudioContext-in-tap path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/460-kids-ball-pit/README.md`

- **[/dream/463-terra-gamelan](/dream/463-terra-gamelan)** ‚ÄĒ Terra Gamelan. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 373.

  **Every earthquake on Earth, right now, ringing a bell tuned to a Javanese gamelan scale on a slowly turning globe of light.** Press **Begin** and the piece continuously fetches the **live USGS real-time earthquake feed** (`all_day.geojson`, CORS-public, no auth, no API route ‚ÄĒ fetched directly client-side) and turns each quake into a tuned bell on a rotating luminous three.js Earth. **depth ‚Üí pitch** (shallow rings high, deep rings low, quantized to **slendro** or **pelog**), **magnitude ‚Üí loudness + decay + flash brightness** (micro-quakes are soft ticks, M5+ are resonant gongs), **longitude ‚Üí stereo pan + position on the globe**. It is stateful and long-form: the last ~24h of quakes accumulate as glowing depth-colored embers (warm=shallow, cool=deep) on a Fibonacci dot-globe, and an ever-present **drone bed** brightens/thickens with rolling global seismic energy, so a seismically busy hour sounds fuller. The instrument is the planet; the score is the Earth's own trembling. This is **cycle 1 of the new "Living Earth" spine** ‚ÄĒ the jury's (2026-06-09) #1 ask realized: *restart a multi-cycle commitment* (nothing had since 414), and it breaks the recent adult **latent-image monoculture** (AI-image OUT was count-banned at 4√ó).

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **live seismic ingest** (`seismic.ts`) ‚ÄĒ USGS GeoJSON fetch with abort+timeout, id-diff for new arrivals, a rolling-energy window, and a complete **synthetic fallback** (Gutenberg-Richter magnitudes + Poisson arrivals + a 24h backlog seeded around tectonic hotspots) so the piece is a full, beautiful instrument with **no network** (what the build check sees). (2) **gamelan bell synth** (`audio.ts`) ‚ÄĒ `GamelanEngine`: slendro `[0,231,474,717,955]` / pelog `[0,120,258,539,675,785,943]` cents over a low-D gong base; inharmonic bonang/gong partials (1 / 2.41 / 3.93) + a band-passed noise-burst mallet attack, fast attack / exponential decay; **everything ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor (‚ąí18/4:1) ‚Üí brick-wall limiter (‚ąí2 dB/20:1)** so a swarm can never clip or hurt ears. (3) **evolving drone bed** ‚ÄĒ sustained mildly-inharmonic partials whose level + lowpass cutoff glide with rolling seismic energy; a baseline hum keeps it **never silent**. (4) **three.js globe** (`scene.ts`) ‚ÄĒ Fibonacci dot-sphere + faint wireframe + occluding core + additive atmosphere halo + 1400-star field; quake embers in a single additive **Points buffer with a custom soft-sprite size shader**, flash-pulse on ring, depth-color, 24h TTL + 500 cap with in-place compaction, inertial drag-to-spin, full geometry/material/renderer dispose on unmount. **No API route, no `guard` needed** (public CORS endpoint, read-only, no secrets). No new npm deps (`three` is an existing repo dep).

  References: **arXiv 2602.14560** (*Preliminary sonification of ENSO using traditional Javanese gamelan scales*, Feb 16 2026 ‚ÄĒ parameter-mapping onto pelog/slendro; this prototype applies the same culturally-situated tuning to seismic rather than climate data) ¬∑ **Seismic Sound Lab** / **Ben Holtzman & Anna Barth** (Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory ‚ÄĒ earthquake-data sonification lineage) ¬∑ **Ryoji Ikeda** (data-aesthetic, though the vibe here is warm/resonant, not cold-clinical). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß373. **Ambition 3/5** (honest): **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (4, above) + **#3** named references + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 1 of the Living Earth spine). **#1 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ grep-verified: **slendro** tuning is already in `402-kids-steady-walk` and **pelog** in `408-kids-breath-grove`, and a live external-data stream already exists in `437-wiki-pulse`; the fresh moves are the **planetary/geophysical** data domain + the gamelan-tuned-globe synthesis (application novelty, not a new primitive). **Diversity** (vs cycle bans AI-image-OUT [4√ó count] ¬∑ Canvas2D-OUT ¬∑ drum-machine-TECH ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE ¬∑ calm-pentatonic-kids-template): **live-planetary-data INPUT** ¬∑ **three.js globe + additive shader OUTPUT** (zero Canvas2D, zero AI-image) ¬∑ **gamelan-tuned data-sonification TECHNIQUE** ¬∑ **warm planetary-observatory VIBE**. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-attack adult fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *play the living Earth from a live planetary data stream, tuned to a non-Western system* ‚ÄĒ three attacks: data-source √ó musical-structure √ó renderer); siblings `464-helios-aurora` (NOAA space-weather ‚Üí EDM build-and-drop aurora, Kp‚Č•5 = the drop) + `465-terra-pulse` (USGS seismic energy ‚Üí long-form EDM build-and-drop journey) are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (own folder, pure client-side public-CORS fetch, no API route, synthetic fallback, brick-wall limiter) and banked in IDEAS ¬ß373 as the Living Earth spine's **cycle-2** candidates. **Build-verified (exit 0, prerendered `/dream/463-terra-gamelan/page.js` in the manifest, `npx eslint` on the folder clean), NOT browser-verified** (no live network / GPU / audio in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether USGS CORS + the 60s poll-diff flow from Karel's network, real-GPU rendering of the ember sprite shader across mobile GPUs, audible gamelan-bell + drone-evolution balance, and the iOS AudioContext-in-tap path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/463-terra-gamelan/README.md`

- **[/dream/468-kids-bottle-flute](/dream/468-kids-bottle-flute)** ‚ÄĒ Bottle Flute. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 374. **For**: kids (4+).

  **A row of glowing glass bottles you BLOW into ‚ÄĒ and they're real physically-modeled flutes that breathe, resonate, and overblow to the octave when you blow hard.** Press **Start**, then tap a bottle to pick its note and blow into the mic (or just blow ‚ÄĒ loudness = how hard you blow); no mic, just tap and a preset breath-puff plays the note. Big bottle = low, small = high (C D E G A, a warm C-Lydian pentatonic subset, so every combination is consonant). The sound is **not** a sample or a plain oscillator: it's a digital **waveguide flute** ‚ÄĒ a delay-line bore + one-pole reflection filter, excited by breath-noise through a soft-clip cubic *jet* nonlinearity, so it has a breathy attack, a resonant body, and a genuine overblow (the cubic term shifts the operating point at high pressure ‚Üí the octave emerges, same physics as a real flute). This is **the lab's first physically-modeled WIND instrument** ‚ÄĒ distinct from Karplus‚ÄďStrong (`140`/`184`), which is the simplest *plucked-string* waveguide; this is a *breath-excited bore*, a different physical regime.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 5): (1) **`flute.ts` digital-waveguide DSP** ‚ÄĒ an **AudioWorklet** processor (source inlined as a string ‚Üí Blob URL, so no `public/` file is needed; a **ScriptProcessorNode** fallback runs the identical loop if `addModule` throws): circular delay line of length `N = round(sampleRate/freq)`, one-pole lowpass reflection, `jet = pressure + bore¬∑0.4` soft-clipped by `x ‚ąí x¬≥/3`, feedback < 1 with a hard ¬Ī1.15 clamp + NaN guard so it can never screech. (2) **Breath input** ‚ÄĒ mic RMS envelope (AnalyserNode `getFloatTimeDomainData`, analysis-only, never recorded) drives the `breath` AudioParam; OR a pure-touch preset envelope when there's no mic. (3) **Reverb** ‚ÄĒ a 4-comb Schroeder + 2-allpass network (no deps). (4) **Always-on ambient pad** (C3 E3 G3 triangles + slow LFO) so it's never silent, all through a **DynamicsCompressor** brick-wall limiter (kids-safe). (5) **Inline-SVG visuals** ‚ÄĒ five graduated glowing bottles, `feGaussianBlur` glow + warm-glass `linearGradient`, a breath-shimmer ellipse + rising bubbles that grow with blow strength, animated by rAF DOM-attribute writes (no Canvas2D, no WebGL). Auto-demo blows a gentle 15-note tune hands-free ~3 s after load.

  References: **Julius O. Smith III**, *Physical Audio Signal Processing* (CCRMA ‚ÄĒ waveguide bore modeling, reflection filters) ¬∑ **Perry R. Cook**, *Real Sound Synthesis for Interactive Applications* (2002) + the **STK Flute** model (jet delay + breath-noise architecture) ¬∑ **McIntyre, Schumacher & Woodhouse**, "On the oscillations of musical instruments" (JASA 1983 ‚ÄĒ the nonlinear-exciter + linear-resonator framing). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß374 (the dive that bound this fire **off** the GPU-physics-sim kids monoculture toward embodied breath/voice). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (first **breath-excited bore waveguide / physically-modeled WIND** instrument in the lab ‚ÄĒ grep-verified: prior "waveguide" hits `140`/`184` are Karplus‚ÄďStrong *plucked strings*, a different regime) + **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (waveguide DSP ¬∑ breath-mic envelope ¬∑ touch ¬∑ Schroeder reverb ¬∑ SVG render = 5) + **#3** named references (Smith ¬∑ Cook/STK ¬∑ McIntyre et al. 1983). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the refs are foundational, not <14-day. **Diversity** (vs the cycle's bans ‚ÄĒ AI-image OUT [4√ó] ¬∑ Canvas2D OUT [jury] ¬∑ **GPU-physics-sim TECH** [451/456/460 = 3 straight kids cycles] ¬∑ drum-machine TECH [jury] ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve & pentatonic-lullaby VIBE [jury]): **breath/mic-RMS + touch INPUT** (fresh ‚ÄĒ amplitude, not pitch, not tilt) ¬∑ **inline-SVG OUTPUT** (dodges BOTH the jury-banned Canvas2D AND the 5√ó WebGL-render monoculture) ¬∑ **digital-waveguide physical-modeling TECHNIQUE** (a brand-new audio primitive ‚ÄĒ dodges both GPU-physics-sim and drum-machine) ¬∑ **warm breathy resolving Lydian VIBE** (not a pentatonic lullaby). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire fired deliberately off the GPU-physics rut; siblings `466-kids-sing-kite` (SING to fly a kite along a scrolling melody contour, YIN pitch-detect, SVG) + `467-kids-shadow-band` (WAVE your body ‚ÄĒ camera motion-energy conducts a 3-zone particle band, three.js) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬ß374. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `‚úď Compiled successfully`, prerendered `/dream/468-kids-bottle-flute` 6.96 kB in the manifest; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean), NOT browser-verified** (no mic / audio / real hardware in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether a 4-year-old's breath drives the RMS envelope into the overblow regime (`env‚Čą0.75‚Äď0.85`) at real mic gain/distance; AudioWorklet Blob-URL loading on iOS Safari (the ScriptProcessor fallback + touch-only path cover it); whether the waveguide *feels* breathy/flute-like vs. buzzy on phone speakers behind the limiter; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture + getUserMedia-in-gesture. No API route, no network, no camera, no secrets ‚Üí no `guard` needed. Zero new npm deps.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/468-kids-bottle-flute/README.md`

- **[/dream/471-helios-orbit](/dream/471-helios-orbit)** ‚ÄĒ Helios ‚ÄĒ Orbit. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 375.

  **Watch a dark Earth from orbit while a live geomagnetic storm conducts an EDM build-and-drop ‚ÄĒ the auroral ovals igniting and the music resolving the instant the storm peaks.** Press **Begin** and three live **NOAA SWPC** feeds (solar-wind plasma, IMF Bz, planetary Kp) stream in client-side: **wind speed ‚Üí master filter brightness + tempo**, **density ‚Üí pad shimmer/thickness**, **Bz-negative ‚Üí harmonic tension** (the pad drifts A-major ‚Üí minor/suspended), **Kp ‚Üí the master anticipation** (a riser sweep + quickening 16th-note hi-hats build). At **Kp ‚Č• 5 the DROP fires exactly once** (a `prevStormActive` one-shot): a sub-bass swell + a 4-on-the-floor kick at 128 BPM + the harmony **snaps minor‚Üímajor ‚ÄĒ it resolves on purpose** + the **auroral ovals ignite** (colatitude expands ~25¬į‚Üí40¬į toward the equator, hue greens‚Üímagenta/red as in real oxygen/nitrogen storm physics) + the **magnetosphere blooms** (an additive halo surge). Then it eases back to a warm ambient bed. Stateful, hands-free, **never silent**; a scripted ~22-s storm auto-fires (and a **"Simulate storm now"** button forces it) so it has a guaranteed build‚Üídrop payoff with no network. This is **cycle 2 of the "Living Earth" spine** (cycle 1 = `463-terra-gamelan`) ‚ÄĒ it keeps a multi-cycle commitment alive (jury 2026-06-09 #1) and carries forward `463`'s luminous Fibonacci-dot-globe visual language, while delivering Karel's explicitly-wanted **EDM build-and-drop journey-engine alternative** and the jury's missing **"warm AND structured AND resolves on purpose"** middle.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí ~4): (1) **live space-weather ingest** (`spaceweather.ts`) ‚ÄĒ three NOAA SWPC public-CORS JSON feeds, 60-s poll with `AbortController` + 8-s timeout, exponential glide (~0.92/s) so values breathe rather than step; a complete **synthetic random-walk + scripted-storm fallback** (quiet ‚Üí build ‚Üí Kp‚Č•6.5 drop ‚Üí decay over ~66 s) so the piece is a full instrument with **no network** (what the build check sees); NaN-guarded parsing ‚Üí silent synthetic fallback on any fetch/CORS/schema failure. (2) **EDM build-and-drop audio engine** (`audio.ts`) ‚ÄĒ warm A-major pad bed, minor/suspended drift on Bz-negative, riser + hi-hat accumulator on the Kp build, sub-bass + 4-on-the-floor kick + major-chord resolve + aurora-bloom chord on the drop; everything ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` ‚Üí brick-wall limiter (20:1, ‚ąí1 dBFS) so nothing clips or is silent. (3) **three.js orbital globe** (`scene.ts`) ‚ÄĒ dark Fibonacci dot-sphere + additive atmosphere halo (continuity with `463`); **auroral ovals** as 1,200-particle shimmer-rings around the IGRF magnetic poles (~80.7¬įN/72¬įW, ~64.5¬įS/136¬įE) whose colatitude/brightness/hue track Kp (70%) + Bz-southward (30%); a **magnetosphere bloom** shell (2.8√ó globe) + corona that `triggerBloom()` flashes then exp-decays on the drop; drag-to-rotate, slow auto-rotation, full geometry/material/renderer dispose on unmount; WebGL-absent ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice + audio still runs. **No API route, no `guard`** (public CORS, read-only, no secrets). No new npm deps (`three` is an existing repo dep).

  References: **arXiv 2605.21874** (Alunno & Bientinesi, *Real-time, EDM-inspired sonification*, **May 21 2026** ‚ÄĒ EDM's build-tension-release as a continuous, fatigue-resistant monitoring sonification grammar; the basis for the build-and-drop arc; carried from RESEARCH ¬ß373) ¬∑ **NASA HARP** (Heliophysics Audified: Resonances in Plasmas) + **NASA/Chandra aurora sonifications** (Mar 2026 ‚ÄĒ brightness/position/energy ‚Üí pitch/volume/timbre) ¬∑ **NOAA SWPC Aurora Dashboard (Experimental)** (live data + auroral-oval colatitude-expansion lineage) ¬∑ **IGRF** (magnetic-pole coordinates) ¬∑ **Refik Anadol** / **Ryoji Ikeda** (data-as-light, but warm). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß375 (the dive bound the build via path (b)/(c) ‚ÄĒ continue a research-seeded multi-cycle spine). **Ambition 4/5**: **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (live ingest + EDM engine + orbital-globe-with-ovals) + **#3** named refs (above) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 2 of the Living Earth spine) + **#5** carried ¬ß373 research within 14 days (arXiv 2605.21874, the EDM arc). **#1 NOT claimed ‚ÄĒ grep-honest:** a live-NOAA-space-weather aurora already exists (`262-aurora-particle`‚Ě§ÔłŹ, the lab's 2nd real-world-data sonification); the fresh moves are the **EDM build-and-drop structural arc** (262 is an ambient drone), the **orbital-globe vantage** (262 is sky-facing curtains), and the **spine framing** ‚ÄĒ application novelty, not a new primitive. **Diversity** (cycle bans: AI-image OUT [4√ó count] ¬∑ Canvas2D OUT [jury] ¬∑ drum-machine TECH [jury] ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve & pentatonic-lullaby VIBE [jury]): **live-space-weather INPUT** ¬∑ **three.js orbital-globe + auroral-ovals OUTPUT** (zero Canvas2D, zero AI-image; the globe is **declared spine continuity with `463`**, not an unintentional rut ‚ÄĒ the one diversity caveat, accepted because a multi-cycle spine extending its own visual language is the #4 commitment the jury asked for) ¬∑ **EDM build-and-drop data-sonification TECHNIQUE** (warm, resolves ‚ÄĒ not drum-machine) ¬∑ **warm space-observatory-that-resolves VIBE** (the jury's missing middle). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-attack adult fire (one Helios concept ‚ÄĒ *NOAA space-weather ‚Üí EDM build-and-drop, storm = the drop* ‚ÄĒ three renderers: orbital globe vs. fbm sky-curtain vs. volumetric raymarch). **The grep-audit overturned the first instinct:** siblings `469-helios-aurora` (three.js fbm sky-curtain veil) + `470-helios-raymarch` (raw WebGL2 volumetric raymarch) were complete and real but **NOT banked** ‚ÄĒ both duplicate the register of the loved `262-aurora-particle`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (whose own banked siblings are literally `aurora-raymarch` + `aurora-drone-field`), so re-seeding them would re-seed the "too similar" rut; `471` won precisely because the orbital globe is the **one approach visually distinct from 262** AND the only one that continues the `463` spine. Rides loved `262-aurora-particle`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (here as a *caution*, not a pull) + `321-spectral-flight`‚Ě§ÔłŹ + `323-latent-condensation`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (luminous data-light). **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/471-helios-orbit` 8.96 kB prerendered as static in the manifest; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean), NOT browser-verified** (no live network / GPU / audio in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: NOAA SWPC CORS + the assumed column indices (plasma `[t,density,speed,temp]`, mag Bz idx 3, Kp idx 1; all NaN-guarded ‚Üí synthetic fallback if wrong) from Karel's network; whether the auroral-oval colatitude rebuild (>1.5¬į ‚Üí particle regen) shimmers on a fast synthetic ramp (only ~1√ó/min on real 60-s polling); the kick/sub/pad balance behind the limiter; the iOS AudioContext-in-tap path; whether the orbital globe reads as clearly distinct from `262` on a real GPU.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/471-helios-orbit/README.md`

- **[/dream/474-kids-scan-bloom](/dream/474-kids-scan-bloom)** ‚ÄĒ Scan Bloom ūüĆł. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 376. **For**: kids (4+).

  **Squeeze a soft glowing flower and HEAR its outline sing ‚ÄĒ the shape you see IS the waveform you hear.** Press **Start**, then tap a petal: it rings a warm note (C-major / Lydian, C3‚ÄďC5 ‚ÄĒ every petal consonant) AND the bloom's outline wobbles, then slowly relaxes back into a calm round hum. This is the lab's **first SCANNED SYNTHESIS** instrument (Max Mathews, Bill Verplank, Rob Shaw, "Scanned Synthesis," ICMC 2000): instead of a static wavetable, the oscillator reads a **slowly-vibrating physical ring of 128 coupled masses** (closed toroidal topology, the original Mathews form). A phase accumulator scans that ring at audio rate ‚ÄĒ `scanFreq` is the pitch ‚ÄĒ so the deforming radial outline on screen is literally the live waveform. Poking it injects a Gaussian displacement bump; light damping dissipates the higher harmonics over a few seconds, so a squeeze brightens the timbre and then **resolves on its own** back toward a near-sine. It is **never silent** (a quiet seeded sinusoid hums at rest) and **never clips/hurts ears** (everything through a DynamicsCompressor brick-wall limiter). This is **cycle 1 of the new "Living Wavetable" kids spine** ‚ÄĒ the jury's (2026-06-09) #2 ask realized for the kids lane: *restart a multi-cycle commitment* (#4 was dead since 414).

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **scanned-synthesis AudioWorklet** (`bloom-worklet-src.ts` / `bloom-audio.ts`) ‚ÄĒ a `BloomProcessor` owning `r[128]`/`v[128]` in the audio thread, stepping the closed mass-spring ring once per 128-sample render quantum (`Kn=0.25, Kc=0.002, damp=0.04, dt=1`, circular neighbor coupling) and synthesizing each output sample by **linear-interpolated circular scan** at `scanFreq/sampleRate`; inlined as a string ‚Üí Blob URL ‚Üí `addModule`, with a **ScriptProcessorNode fallback** running the identical loop, posting `r[]` snapshots out ~33√ó/sec; all ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor limiter. (2) **multi-touch petal control** ‚ÄĒ 6 invisible ‚Č•72px tap-targets ringed around the bloom that each poke the ring at their angular index AND set the scan pitch in one gesture; a squeeze/drag anywhere on the canvas rings the *nearest* petal (angle-based hit test). (3) **WebGL2 bloom renderer** (`bloom-renderer.ts`) ‚ÄĒ a two-pass glow (triangle-fan radial-gradient fill + additive LINE_STRIP rim) whose outline radius at each angle = `baseRadius + r[i]¬∑amp`, rebuilt every frame from the exact `r[]` being scanned, warm per-petal color, on a dark `#0a0514` field; ResizeObserver-driven sizing + full GL dispose on unmount. (4) **3s hands-free auto-demo** (taps a gentle 7-note tune, stops on first touch) so it demos with zero interaction. **No API route, no `guard`** (Web Audio + WebGL2 only ‚ÄĒ no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Max V. Mathews, Bill Verplank, Rob Shaw ‚ÄĒ "Scanned Synthesis," Proceedings of the ICMC 2000, Berlin** (the technique: a slow dynamical wavetable scanned at audio rate; this is the lab's first use, closed-toroidal form). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß376 (the dive surfaced the Continuator/MIROR reflexive-turn-taking lineage, a grep-audit killed it as a "too-similar" target ‚ÄĒ `251-live-duet-trader` already does live Markov, and 142/154/213/230/280/298/334 are the echo cluster ‚ÄĒ and **steered the search onto scanned synthesis, a grep-verified untouched primitive**). **Ambition 4/5** (the jury's named regression fix ‚ÄĒ zero pieces were at 4‚Äď5/5): **#1** never-used technique (scanned synthesis ‚ÄĒ grep-verified: "scanned synthesis"/"verplank"/"wavetable" all absent across 470+ folders) + **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (4, above) + **#3** named reference (Mathews/Verplank/Shaw ICMC 2000) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 1 of the Living Wavetable spine). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the technique is foundational (2000), not <14 days; honest. **Diversity** (vs jury 2026-06-09 bans Canvas2D-OUTPUT ¬∑ drum-machine/percussion-synth-TECH ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE ¬∑ calm-cosmic-pentatonic-kids-template ¬∑ AND the recent kids GPU-physics-sim rut [451/456/460] ¬∑ the second-consecutive-SVG [468]): **multi-touch petal INPUT** ¬∑ **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (dodges the banned Canvas2D AND the three.js cluster [471/463/451] AND a 2nd SVG) ¬∑ **scanned-synthesis TECHNIQUE** (a brand-new audio primitive ‚ÄĒ dodges drum-machine AND the kids physics-sim-game rut: the mass-spring here is a tiny audio *wavetable*, not a physics toy you watch) ¬∑ **warm self-resolving Lydian VIBE** (the squeeze brightens then relaxes ‚ÄĒ the jury's "warm AND structured AND resolves on purpose" middle, neither refuse-to-resolve nor pentatonic-lullaby). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-attack kids fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *poke a glowing object and it sings its own shape* ‚ÄĒ three topologies/renderers: open-string/SVG vs. toroidal-ring/three.js vs. radial-bloom/WebGL2). Siblings `472-kids-scan-string` (pluck a giant SVG harp-string of light, pluck-x = pitch ‚ÄĒ the most legible "see the wave" read) + `473-kids-scan-ring` (poke a three.js toroidal halo, the dent travels around ‚ÄĒ the most reference-pure topology) are complete, build-reviewed real scanned-synthesis implementations banked in IDEAS ¬ß376 as the spine's cycle-2/3 candidates. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `‚úď Compiled successfully`, `/dream/474-kids-scan-bloom` 6.35 kB prerendered static in the manifest), NOT browser-verified** (no audio / GPU / touch in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the scanned-synthesis timbre reads as warm-and-alive vs. buzzy behind the limiter on phone speakers; whether a 4yo perceives the see-shape/hear-shape correspondence at low pitch (the ~33fps `r[]` snapshot averages sub-period detail below ~150 Hz, though the complex‚Üísmooth *trend* always mirrors); the iOS AudioWorklet Blob-URL + AudioContext-in-gesture path (ScriptProcessor fallback covers it); WebGL2 availability on older tablets (DOM-bloom + audio fallback covers it); whether free-drag "squeeze" lands on the petal the child intended (angle-based nearest-petal, polished this cycle from an always-C4 bug).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/474-kids-scan-bloom/README.md`

- **[/dream/475-resonant-room](/dream/475-resonant-room)** ‚ÄĒ Resonant Room ūüŹõÔłŹ. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 377. **For**: adults / live performance.

  **Karel's actual recorded piano played into a room that rings back in the SAME KEY ‚ÄĒ hold "Swell" and the space fills with a warm in-key halo; release and it rings out to the tonic and decays to silence.** Press **Start** (or wait ‚ÄĒ 3s hands-free auto-start), pick a **Key** (C/D/E/F/G/A/Bb), and **hold the Swell control** (or Space): the feedback gain `g` ramps toward 0.95, the room blooms, a golden-amber lattice lights up; **release** and `g` ramps to 0 so the room **resolves to the tonic and fades to silence** ‚ÄĒ the defining gesture of this new spine. This is the lab's **first FEEDBACK DELAY NETWORK** (Jot/Stautner‚ÄďPuckette reverb): an N=8 lossless **Householder-matrix** feedback network of delay lines whose lengths are **tuned to the harmonic series of the chosen key** (`L = SR/freq` + a mutually-prime nudge), with a **per-line one-pole lowpass** for Jot-style frequency-dependent warmth (highs decay faster, like a real room). Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano is the excitation (read-only `/api/audio/[id]` GET); on any fetch/decode failure it falls back to a **resolving C‚ÄďAm‚ÄďF‚ÄďG7‚ÄďCmaj9** synth progression so it always sounds and always demos. This is **cycle 1 of the new adult "Resonant Room" multi-cycle spine** ‚ÄĒ the jury's (2026-06-09) **warm-AND-structured-AND-resolves-on-purpose adult middle**, on his **real piano** (cold 15+ cycles), restarting the multi-cycle commitment (#4) the jury said died at 414.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4): (1) **real-piano source** ‚ÄĒ fetch `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶` ‚Üí `decodeAudioData` ‚Üí looping `AudioBufferSourceNode`, with a warm synthesized **fallback chord progression** (C‚ÄďAm‚ÄďF‚ÄďG7‚ÄďCmaj9) that itself resolves. (2) **FDN reverb AudioWorklet** (`fdn-worklet-src.ts`) ‚ÄĒ `FdnProcessor` owning 8 ring-buffer delay lines, mixing tails through a precomputed **Householder matrix** `H = I ‚ąí (2/N)¬∑11ŠĶÄ`, scaling by global feedback `g` (k-rate param, ‚Č§0.97 for stability), per-line warm LP, injecting the dry source, posting per-line **RMS energy** ~40√ó/sec; inlined ‚Üí Blob URL ‚Üí `addModule`, with a **ScriptProcessorNode fallback** running the identical loop; all ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor brick-wall limiter. (3) **WebGL2 ring-lattice renderer** ‚ÄĒ 8 glowing nodes (one per delay line) connected by the mixing-matrix edges; node brightness/size = that line's current RMS, edges pulse as energy flows; golden-amber additive glow on deep indigo `#0a0514`; `ResizeObserver` sizing + full GL dispose on unmount; DOM fallback if WebGL2 absent. (4) **interaction** ‚ÄĒ 6-key selector (retunes delays live), Space/pointer **Swell** gesture (the `g` ramp), 3s auto-start. **No API route, no `guard`** (Web Audio + WebGL2 + one read-only same-origin `/api/audio` GET ‚ÄĒ no mic, camera, write, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Jean-Marc Jot & Antoine Chaigne, "Digital Delay Networks for Designing Artificial Reverberators," AES 90th Convention, 1991** + **Stautner & Puckette, "Designing Multichannel Reverberators," Computer Music Journal, 1982** (the original FDN). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß377 (the dive ruled out the freshest concepts ‚ÄĒ Live Music Diffusion Models, arXiv 2605.22717, 2026-05-21, and latent-diffusion co-performance ‚ÄĒ as server-heavy / not client-buildable, **negatively steering** onto the jury's real-piano + warm-resolving asks realized via a lab-first FDN). **Ambition 4/5** (the jury's named regression fix ‚ÄĒ zero pieces were at 4‚Äď5/5): **#1** never-used technique (Feedback Delay Network ‚ÄĒ grep-verified: "feedback delay network"/"FDN"/"Jot" all absent across 470+ folders; architecturally distinct from 468's single Schroeder *allpass*, the modal resonators of 202/284/286, and the Karplus combs of 140/184) + **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (4, above) + **#3** named reference (Jot & Chaigne 1991 / Stautner & Puckette 1982) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 1 of the Resonant Room spine). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ FDN is foundational (1982/1991), not <14 days; honest. **Diversity** (vs jury 2026-06-09 bans Canvas2D-OUTPUT ¬∑ drum-machine/percussion-synth-TECH ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE ¬∑ calm-cosmic-pentatonic-kids-template ¬∑ AND the over-used three.js cluster [471/463/451]): **audio-file(real WH piano) INPUT** (first real-piano source in 16 cycles) ¬∑ **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (dodges banned Canvas2D AND the three.js cluster AND a repeat-SVG) ¬∑ **feedback-delay-network TECHNIQUE** (a brand-new reverb architecture ‚ÄĒ dodges drum-machine) ¬∑ **warm in-key resolving VIBE** (hold-to-swell ‚Üí release rings out to the tonic ‚ÄĒ the jury's "warm AND structured AND resolves on purpose" adult middle, neither refuse-to-resolve nor pentatonic-lullaby). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept ‚ÄĒ *real piano ‚Üí an in-key sympathetic room that resolves* ‚ÄĒ three resonance architectures: FDN/WebGL2 vs. biquad-resonator-bank/three.js vs. tuned-comb-waveguide/WebGL2). Siblings `476-tarab-strings` (high-Q biquad sympathetic-resonator bank ‚Üí three.js light-strings ‚ÄĒ the most legible per-string energy portrait) + `477-resonant-caustics` (tuned comb/waveguide bank ‚Üí WebGL2 caustic interference field ‚ÄĒ the most beautiful render) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS ¬ß377 as the spine's cycle-2/3 candidates. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `‚úď Compiled successfully`, `/dream/475-resonant-room` 8.7 kB prerendered static), NOT browser-verified** (no audio / GPU in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the FDN reads as a warm sympathetic *room* vs. metallic/comby behind the limiter on phone speakers; whether the in-key delay tuning produces an audible *resolution to the tonic* on Swell-release vs. a generic reverb tail; whether `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶` actually streams + decodes in the deployed prototype (synth fallback covers silence, but the real-piano directive is only *met* if it loads); the iOS AudioWorklet Blob-URL + AudioContext-in-gesture path (ScriptProcessor fallback covers it); WebGL2 on older tablets (DOM fallback + audio-without-GL covers it).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/475-resonant-room/README.md`

- **[/dream/478-kids-wave-pond](/dream/478-kids-wave-pond)** ‚ÄĒ Wave Pond ūüĆä. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 378. **For**: kids (4+).

  **Tap a glowing top-down pond and watch REAL waves spread, bounce off the rim, and cross through each other ‚ÄĒ and hear that rippling field as sound.** Press **Touch the Pond**, then tap/drag anywhere on the luminous circle: a Gaussian displacement pulse is injected into a 64√ó64 grid, ripples radiate, **reflect off the fixed boundary**, and **interfere** where they cross (constructive = brighter/cyan, destructive = dimmer/violet). A small amber **pickup** cell reads the local displacement out as audio, and the field's liveliness drives a warm C-pentatonic tone over an always-on Lydian pad ‚ÄĒ so the audio and the visual are the *same solved field*, not a sim layered on a separate synth. Multi-touch: several taps = several wave sources crossing. This is **cycle 1 of the new "Wave Field" kids spine**.

  Subsystems (‚Č•3 ‚Üí 4+): (1) **FDTD 2-D wave solver** ‚ÄĒ a genuine time-domain finite-difference 2-D digital-waveguide mesh (Van Duyne & Smith, ICMC 1993): every cell steps `u_next = 2u ‚ąí u_prev + c¬≤¬∑(‚ąá¬≤u)` (c = 0.35 Courant-stable, light damping, Dirichlet "rim" boundaries) ‚ÄĒ **no pre-computed modes, no spectral bank, no cosmetic ripple**; propagation/reflection/interference emerge from the coupled cell updates alone. (2) **AudioWorklet** owning the grid, stepping at audio rate and posting the field to the main thread ~30√ó/s (inlined ‚Üí Blob URL, no `public/` file), with an **identical ScriptProcessorNode fallback** if `addModule` throws. (3) **audio chain** ‚ÄĒ pickup-cell displacement + a field-energy-driven C4‚ÄďA4 pentatonic sine voice + a 5-partial Lydian pad (never silent) ‚Üí 9 kHz lowpass (kids-safe) ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor brick-wall limiter. (4) **three.js renderer** ‚ÄĒ top-down `OrthographicCamera` over a `PlaneGeometry` (64√ó64 vertices) whose per-vertex `color` BufferAttribute is rewritten each frame from the field (trough = deep violet, crest = cyan‚Üíwhite) on a dark indigo field; full geometry/material/renderer dispose on unmount; WebGL-absent ‚Üí readable `text-rose-300` notice + audio still runs. (5) **3 s hands-free auto-demo** drops four gentle ripples, cancelled on first touch; multi-touch independent injection; iOS AudioContext created inside the Start gesture. **No API route, no `guard`** (Web Audio + three.js only ‚ÄĒ no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps (`three` is an existing repo dep).

  References: **Dana C. Van Duyne & Julius O. Smith III, "Physical Modeling with the 2-D Digital Waveguide Mesh," ICMC 1993** (the technique ‚ÄĒ a solved 2-D wave-equation field, not a modal mode-bank). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß378 (the dive's grep-audit killed three would-be re-treads ‚ÄĒ bowed-string already exists at `320-kids-light-loom`, kids-weather at `293-kids-sky-band`, modal membrane-drum at `202`/`284` ‚ÄĒ and surfaced the genuinely-open FDTD time-domain field). **Ambition 4/5** (the jury's named regression fix ‚ÄĒ climb to 4‚Äď5/5): **#1** never-used technique (time-domain FDTD 2-D wave-equation / digital-waveguide-mesh ‚ÄĒ grep-verified: the lab's membranes `202`/`284` are *modal* Bessel-mode banks with a cosmetic ripple, never a solved finite-difference field) + **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (4+, above) + **#3** named reference (Van Duyne & Smith 1993) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 1 of the Wave Field spine). **#5 NOT claimed** ‚ÄĒ the reference is foundational (1993), not <14 days; honest. **Diversity** (vs jury 2026-06-09 bans Canvas2D-OUTPUT ¬∑ drum-machine/percussion-synth-TECH ¬∑ refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE ¬∑ calm-cosmic-pentatonic-kids-template ¬∑ AND the count-banned **WebGL2 OUTPUT** [5√ó in last-10: 475/474/460/456/451]): **multi-touch INPUT** ¬∑ **three.js vertex-color OUTPUT** (dodges the banned Canvas2D AND the count-banned raw-WebGL2; three.js is 2√ó ‚ÄĒ under the count line) ¬∑ **FDTD wave-field TECHNIQUE** (a brand-new physical-modeling primitive ‚ÄĒ dodges drum-machine AND the recent kids GPU-physics-sim rut: this is a *solved acoustic field you hear*, not a physics toy you watch) ¬∑ **warm self-resolving water VIBE** (ripples spread then settle ‚ÄĒ neither refuse-to-resolve nor pentatonic-lullaby). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire fired off three grep-verified-fresh registers: siblings `479-kids-duet-bloom` (first cooperative two-player kids instrument ‚ÄĒ the chord only blooms when *both* play together; dual-input synchrony engine, SVG) + `480-kids-sing-bird` (SING and your pitch flies a bird along a melody contour; real-time YIN voice-pitch, SVG) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS ¬ß378. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/478-kids-wave-pond` prerendered in the manifest; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU / audio / touch in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether a 64√ó64 grid stepped at 44.1 kHz in an AudioWorklet holds real-time on a low-end phone (builder flags it ~180 MFLOP/s ‚ÄĒ a production version would sub-rate the stepping or shrink the grid; ScriptProcessor fallback is heavier); whether the raw single-cell pickup reads as a pleasing "water-strike" vs. a low rumble behind the limiter (the pentatonic tone-synth layer carries the pitched warmth); whether tap-to-sound lands <50 ms (injection bypasses React state via a ref); the iOS AudioWorklet Blob-URL + AudioContext-in-gesture path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/478-kids-wave-pond/README.md`

- **[/dream/486-third-room](/dream/486-third-room)** ‚ÄĒ Third Room ūüŹõÔłŹ. `demoable` ¬∑ Cycle 381. **For**: adults / live performance.

  **Cycle 3 of the Resonant Room spine: a live-MIDI room split into a tritone clash ‚ÄĒ but now with a THIRD room a just-fifth above that shares Room A's harmonics, so you can negotiate the tritone into rest by routing it through a consonant intermediary, not just collapsing it.** Every played note feeds three parallel banks of N=8 high-Q biquad bandpass resonators: **Room A** (reference, panned ‚ąí20¬į L, violet), **Room B** (the tritone interloper, +detune 0‚Äď600 ¬Ę, default 600 ¬Ę, +35¬į R, rose), and **Room C** (a **just-fifth, 702 ¬Ę = 3:2** above A, ‚ąí35¬į L, emerald ‚ÄĒ its 2nd/4th/6th partials lock onto A's 3rd/6th/9th, so A‚ÜĒC is intrinsically consonant). A **Room C blend slider** brings the bridge in from silence; the resolution gesture is *blend C in ‚Üí find the pivot note where all three rooms momentarily agree ‚Üí then collapse the detune*. A real-time **Plomp‚ÄďLevelt / Sethares roughness** engine (computed over all cross-room partial pairs, C weighted by its blend) drives a Tension bar (Resolved ‚Üí Settling ‚Üí Tense ‚Üí Dissonant ‚Üí Clashing) and a **steepest-descent pivot-note hint layer** that analytically scans MIDI 48‚Äď84 each ~18 frames and glows the 2‚Äď3 notes that most lower total three-room roughness emerald on the keyboard ‚ÄĒ a partner that whispers crossroads without forcing one. Rendered as a **WebGPU compute vorticity field** (the lab's starved renderer): ~4,000 particles in a WGSL ping-pong compute shader, three room-attractors, roughness ‚Üí per-room vorticity (Room C calms to laminar emerald as you find consonance while A‚ÜĒB stays turbulent at full tritone), with a real Canvas2D three-attractor fallback. Live Web MIDI + on-screen + QWERTY input; 3-s hands-free auto-demo (plays at full tritone then blends C in); brick-wall limiter; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client-side Web Audio + Web MIDI + WebGPU/Canvas2D ‚ÄĒ no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Plomp & Levelt 1965** / **Sethares 1993** / **MacCallum & Einbond, "Real-Time Analysis of Sensory Dissonance," CMMR 2008** (the roughness model) ¬∑ **Stautner & Puckette 1982** / **Jot & Chaigne 1991** (FDN/tuned-resonator lineage) ¬∑ **just intonation 3:2 = 702 ¬Ę** (the Pythagorean fifth ‚ÄĒ the consonant bridge). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß381 (the dive confirmed no fresh <14-day sensory-dissonance paper ‚ÄĒ the foundational MacCallum‚ÄďEinbond real-time roughness model stays the right tool ‚ÄĒ and positively bound the renderer: WebGPU compute particle fields with ping-pong buffers [ShaderVine's 16 compute sims, nibi's TSL flow patterns] are the live 2026 register). **Ambition 4/5**: **#2** ‚Č•3 subsystems (live Web MIDI + **three** coupled resonator rooms + real-time roughness analysis + steepest-descent pivot-note suggestion engine + WebGPU compute vorticity field = 5) + **#3** named refs (above) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 3 of the Resonant Room spine ‚ÄĒ the truest *extension*: one room [475] ‚Üí two [483] ‚Üí three) + (#5 honestly NOT claimed ‚ÄĒ refs foundational, no <14-day binding; #1 NOT claimed ‚ÄĒ WebGPU compute exists at `444`, roughness at `483`). **This directly answers the JURY (2026-06-10): #1** bring genuine tension the listener resolves *through action* (blend ‚Üí pivot ‚Üí collapse, never auto-resolved), **#2** *deepen* an existing lab-first into cycle 3 by extension rather than grabbing a 5th DSP primitive, **#3** ban three.js + pick a starved renderer (**WebGPU**, 0√ó in the last 10; dodges the WebGL2 4√ó count-ban). **Diversity** `picked=[**live-MIDI INPUT** (starved, jury's pick, spine continuity) ¬∑ **WebGPU compute OUTPUT** (0√ó starved, jury-flagged; dodges banned three.js + WebGL2-4√ó count-ban) ¬∑ **three-room tritone-vs-just-fifth negotiation + real-time roughness TECHNIQUE** (a spine *extension*, not a new primitive ‚ÄĒ dodges the jury's "first-DSP-primitive" treadmill) ¬∑ **cold/ambiguous-tension-you-resolve-through-action VIBE** (the jury's exact #1 ask ‚ÄĒ the opposite of the 15√ó warm-consonant monoculture)]`. **Love signal:** `130-tsl-particle-compute`‚Ě§ÔłŹ (a loved GPU-compute particle piece) softly backs the WebGPU vorticity field; `105-pluck-field`‚Ě§ÔłŹ / `140-kids-string-bridge`‚Ě§ÔłŹ back a resonator piece ‚ÄĒ additive, the jury + spine governed. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one cycle-3 concept ‚ÄĒ *deepen 483 with a steepest-descent partner + the starved WebGPU renderer* ‚ÄĒ three resolution mechanics): siblings `485-resolving-room` (the whisper-panel note-hint engine + detune gradient arrow) + `487-guided-descent` (a roughness *landscape* with a shared-control "descend toward rest" autopilot) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS ¬ß381 as cycle-4 fold-ins. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/486-third-room/page` in `app-paths-manifest.json`; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean ‚ÄĒ one unused eslint-disable directive removed as the single fix), NOT browser-verified** (no MIDI device / GPU / audio in this sandbox) ‚ÄĒ unverified surface: whether the analytically-predicted pivot notes actually *sound* resolved to the ear (the Plomp‚ÄďLevelt model is an approximation, not ground truth ‚ÄĒ players may explore a few candidates); whether 3√ó8 high-Q biquads per held note stays clean polyphonically behind the limiter; whether the just-fifth A‚ÜĒC consonance reads as a genuine *bridge* vs. just more sound on phone speakers; `requestMIDIAccess` + hotplug on Karel's controller (on-screen/QWERTY fallback covers no-device); whether WebGPU initializes on Karel's review phone (Canvas2D fallback covers no-`navigator.gpu`); the iOS AudioContext-in-gesture path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/486-third-room/README.md`

### [493-kids-feeling-forest](/dream/493-kids-feeling-forest) ¬∑ kids ¬∑ cycle 384 ¬∑ demoable
  **Cross a glowing "feeling forest" with your BODY ‚ÄĒ every place sounds like a different feeling, and you find calm (or stay in the bittersweet middle) by *moving*, not by pressing a "resolve" button.** The lab's first **explorable continuous harmony-tension MAP**: the screen is a top-down world; a glowing spirit sits at the child's position; **6 mood anchors** ‚ÄĒ Storm (m2+tritone+m9 cluster, 22¬Ę beating, sawtooth, fast tremolo, 380 Hz LP), Electric (aug+dom7), Bittersweet sus2 center, Dream whole-tone, Sweet major, Calm (open fifth+octave, 0¬Ę detune, no tremolo, 2200 Hz) ‚ÄĒ are blended at every position by **inverse-distance-weighting (power 2)**. Chord voicing (fractional semitone offsets ‚ÄĒ no scale-snapping), detune, oscillator timbre, per-voice filter cutoff, and tremolo rate all morph **continuously**, so dissonance‚ÜĒconsonance is a smooth **spatial gradient the child drives by walking**. There is no pre-baked cadence ‚ÄĒ tension is the subject; you resolve it (or dwell in it) through navigation. **Navigation by BODY/camera (primary, the jury's starved depth-camera input):** MediaPipe PoseLandmarker (loaded from CDN at runtime, no npm dep) maps the child's mirrored body/nose position onto the canvas; **drag/pointer fallback** if camera denied (`text-rose-300` banner) or MediaPipe CDN fails; **always-on auto-demo** wanders prickly‚Üíelectric‚Üíbittersweet‚Üísweet‚Üícalm‚Üídreamy (~14 s loop) from page load so the sandbox always makes evolving sound + visuals hands-free; any touch/camera input stops it. **Canvas2D only** (8√ó8 mood-tinted grid ‚ÄĒ cold/jittering where dissonant, warm/steady where calm ‚ÄĒ + glowing creature that vibrates & sprouts spiky rays in tense zones, trail, tension-keyed particles, tiny live camera-position indicator). DynamicsCompressor limiter (‚ąí6 dBFS) so it can never get harsh-loud; AudioContext created inside the Start gesture (iOS). **No API route, no `guard`** (Web Audio + Canvas2D + on-device camera; the only network is the MediaPipe CDN + model; no mic-audio leaves the browser, no secrets). No new npm deps.

  Reference: **Chu, A., Smith, J. B. & Pardo, B., *FXplorer: A Map-Based Interface for Exploratory Audio Effect Design*, arXiv 2606.08286 (NIME 2026, listed 2026-06-09)** ‚ÄĒ replace menus/sliders with a continuous 2-D map you *navigate*; discover sound by *wandering*. This adapts it for pre-readers: the map encodes harmony+tension (not effect params) and the **body** is the cursor. Born from RESEARCH ¬ß384 (the dive *implemented* a <14-day finding ‚ÄĒ the missing 5th criterion the jury said kept every recent piece at 4/5). **Ambition 5/5**: **#1** never-used technique (explorable continuous harmony-tension map navigated by movement ‚ÄĒ grep-verified lab-first;
